title: I Wish I had More Hate in Me
content:
I Wish I had More Hate in Me

Life is easier when things are black and white

Can you live in a world where everything is just ok?

I’ve spent the past 5 years trying to get the concept of hate out of my system.Nothing can be that bad. Hate kills you from inside.

Now I stand at this junction, seriously confused about the meaning of regret, good life, good choices, or happiness. When there is nothing to hate, everything isjust okay. Can you live in a world where everything is always “just ok”?

Is it possible that there are things you are supposed to hate? Hate with passion just so that you can fiercely love other things?

Like choices you can't undo, people you can’t unmeet, love you cannot take back, jobs you’ve stayed too long in, places that don’t make you happy. Is it possible that you have to feel visceral hatred to move on?

The world is a better place than you think. Can it be too good to hate?

I grew up in a culture where being condescending was the only way you can encourage your child to be better. Being afraid, insecure, and not trusting was the default setting when I met new people.

After I moved out of my old life, despite everything I was expecting, the outside world was a very friendly place. There were hundreds of good people, right among the hundreds of terrible ones.

Some of them are better than my best self, even by my own standards. Not because they “achieved” more, but just because they were wonderful human beings. They didn’t tell me that at home. They didn’t know it themselves.

It wasn’t difficult toun-hateonce youun-blindyourself to the good of the world. As long as you keep choosing to not see it, there is nothing good to see. Once you see it, you will not go back.

What’s the problem with not hating?

Now after 5 years of seeing what is good and worth living for, I feel like the Eloi in H.G.Wells’Time Machine.The Eloi, an excerpt fromWikipedia:

With all their needs and desires perfectly fulfilled, the Eloi have slowly become dissolute and naive: they are described as smaller than modern humans, with sub-human intelligence.

They do not perform much work, except to feed, play, and mate, and are characterized by apathy;

Is it possible that getting rid of hate and dislike is the only“desire to be fulfilled”in any life? Would you rather be knocked out of balance once in a while so that you can pursue life which is basically, love, career, and happiness? Banalities as they might be, that is the whole of what life is made up of.

As I write this, I find myself in a state of apathy. Not an inability to move on, but the palpable lack of enough hatred to do so.

I feel the reluctance to attach myself to something and want it badly enough to be devastated If I don’t have it. Badly enough to do the“things we do for love”.

“We are not mad. We are human. We want to love, and someone must forgive us for the paths we take to love, for the paths are many and dark, and we are ardent and cruel in our journey.”

Leonard Cohen

I want to be cruel in my journey. I want to be able to sing odes to my love. Take paths that will hurt. But I don’t have the hate in me to cause the damage. Unfortunately, love doesn’t come aggressively as hate can.

I am Sruthi Korlakunta, what I wrote should have told you who I am.

Read me onMedium, or write me onLinkedIn. If I convinced you to become a member on medium,join here, If I didn’t,read herefor free.

title: 4 Jaw-dropping Books On Medicine For Non-Doctors
content:
4 Jaw-dropping Books On Medicine For Non-Doctors

Holiday presents for those who want to know everything

Do you want to know EVERYTHING? But you don't know where to start? The best way to kick start your adventure in everything is to pick up a book in that genre.

Regardless of what your day-job is, these four books are a brilliant start to exploring the world of the human body and some soul searching.

If you are looking for a perfect holiday present for someone who can’t seem to know enough, any one of these would make a perfect present!

When Breath Becomes Air

by Paul Kalanithi

A rare gift to the world,When Breath Becomes Airis a moving epithet of a neurosurgeon and a human with depth.

The author is a doctor on his death bed, Paul Kalanithi. He was a neurosurgeon who was also a Master of Arts in English Literature and an MD from Yale. That combination can only be an odd coincidence because the readers are stupidly lucky.

He wrote this book while succumbing to lung cancer. Having helped hundreds of patients deal with the possibility of death, Kalanithi finds himself at once on the other side.

As opposed to every patient he dealt with, he knew what to expect from cancer that would consume him in months to come.

This book is a passionate account of his time as a neurosurgeon, a student, a husband, and most of all a human confronted vis-a-vis with the largest fear known to mankind.

It is a moving memoir that you will remember for years to come.

Man who Mistook his Wife for a Hat

by Oliver Sacks

We take for granted a lot of what we are given. The fact that you perceive, comprehend, and respond without as much as a fleeting moment of thought is a wonder. A wonder that everyone experiences differently.

Mysteries of the body have long been understood and find empathy in the society but mysteries of the mind are less understood.

A blind man gets help when he wants to cross the street but when one mentally loses connection with one’s arm or leg while being physically capable? We have no understanding of that predicament.

What if you don’t know anymore what is the concept of “right side” while retaining all information about what something means on the left side?

Such are the questions Neurologist Oliver Sacks deals with, in his book “Man who mistook his wife for a hat”.

Oliver sacks unfolds the mysteries of the mind with the enthusiasm of a child and the skill of a neurologist. He treats illnesses of the mind with empathy and respect to the “human-ness” of his patients, careful not to obfuscate their humanity for their empirical value as a lab subject.

In all, this book is a journey into the mind written by a humble scientist in a warm tone.

Being Mortal

By Atul Gawande

Despite mankind’s copious experience, death is still a very taboo topic. We have successfully learned how to live longer, but we are yet to learn how to die.

Old age, terminal illnesses, and the question of dependence on family or hospice care are considered so dreadful that there is hardly enough work put into that area of research.

In Being Mortal, Atul Gawande addresses these topics with a great balance between objectivity and emotion.

Himself having faced the question in the wake of his father’s illness and having seen many such cases during his tenure as a doctor, Atul asks hard questions and provides soothing options.

Being originally from a culture where homes for the old are taboo, but growing up in western civilization where dependence is equally taboo, Atul understands the sensitivity of the issue and feeds you the bitter medicine with deserving warmth.

This Is Going to Hurt: Secret Diaries of a Junior Doctor

by Adam Kay

I do not have enough stars to give this book. In the genre between humor, information, and medicine, it is the best of its kind.

Adam makes you laugh so hard at one moment you forget that it is not funny when someone is in pain. The book is full of irony, it scares you and makes you laugh.

Adam Kay was a doctor so vexed with the public health system, that he delivered a bazillion babies and then quit to be a humorous screenwriter.

This book is even funnier than the books listed in the humor section. I would really be hard-pressed to find a funnier book I read in the past 5 years.

You just read another post from In Fitness And In Health:a health and fitness community dedicated to sharing knowledge, lessons, and suggestions to living happier, healthier lives.

title: 5 Buys to Put Your Fitness Program in Over Drive
content:
5 Buys to Put Your Fitness Program in Over Drive

Automate your body building

1. A Good watch/Tracker : I have the Garmin Vivoactive 5

My Garmin Vivoactive 5 is my bestfriend. No kidding. It’s like having a personal health assistant.

What I track with it

Sleep and rest

The most important aspect of my watch for me is my sleep pattern and rest quality, since I have difficulty in falling asleep easy. Garmin gamifies the process and incentivises me to sleep more. I love to watch the progress!

2. Calorie turnover

It also seamlessly syncs with my food tracker MyFitnessPal, so I can monitor my calorie intake. Though my trainer told me its not a 100% accurate, I keep an eye on both active and inactive calories with Garmin, helping me make better decisions. I also program my workout programs into it, including rest days and rest minutes between sets, and it guides me through my routines. Before garmin I was hardly 50% as efficient at the Gym.

3. Fitness coaching

A super nifty feature is the Garmin coach that plans my 5–10 week strength training programs with progressive overload. I had also used the running coach in the past and made progress.

4. Performance metrics

Garmin has a webapp that gives me detailed graphs and performance metrics give me a clear picture of my health journey, showing where I’ve improved and what areas need more focus. I track weight, weight I lift, distances I run and bike, VO2 Max, heartrate, fitness snapshot, and so much more.

The device is practically a Beast. It has withstood my swimming, mountain climbs, and tough outdoor adventures without breaking sweat.

If I would only choose one accessory for all my fitness activities, Id choose the watch, even over essentials like gloves and lifting shoes.

2. A Meal Tracker App

Bodies are made in the kitchen.

Keeping an eye on what you eat is pretty mich step 1 if you want to manage your weight and mood. A good meal tracker app makes it super easy to log your meals — you can even scan barcodes for quick entries. It shows you how many calories you’re eating, along with your macros and even the vitamins and minerals. Some apps give you personalized tips based on what you’re aiming for, like losing weight, building muscle, or just eating healthier overall.

I am using MyFitnessPal, and it has the best features, even without having the premium version. Having that info helps you make smarter food choices day in and day out without much hassle.

3. A Prescheduled Workout Plan

Garmin has taken care of this for me, since I can put together a plan that works for me and my schedule. But if you are just showing up at the gym and deciding what to do, you are only going to be half as efficient.

Decide on a workout plan, log everything you are doing and hit every muscle group once or twice a week. Use an app, a device or even a notebook, but dont go to the gym without a plan.

4. A Draw Full of Safe Snacks

No body is safe from a craving or a midnight snack hunger. Having a draw full of safe, healthy snacks can make a big difference to what you reach out to during these weak times.

Stock it with options like rice cakes, veggie sticks, greek yogurt, or whole-grain bars, etc. Make sure to look for minimum calorie maximum protein when shopping for snacks. The more natural they are, the better. I personally avoid buying nuts and dry fruits too often since they are so easy to over eat.

These snacks are not only satisfying but also packed with nutrients, helping you avoid the junk food trap. Plus, portioning out snacks in advance keeps you from overeating.

5. A Shopping list curated when you are not hungry

I just scream out for Alexa when ever I have a thing on my mind for the shopping list. It’s so handy to add things without even pulling out my phone. I’ve learned to wait until I’m not hungry before heading to the store — cravings can totally mess with my choices. Eating a snack or meal beforehand helps me stay focused and stick to my plan. When I shop on a full stomach, I avoid impulse buys like sweets or junk food. Making a list when I’m not hungry keeps me organized and ensures I get only what I need, making healthy choices easier and less stressful.

title: Short Story: Spacecraft Commanders
content:
Short Story: Spacecraft Commanders

Short story with AI-Generated Illustrations from NightCafe Studio.

Little Milo died that morning. 8-year-old Abhi did not shed a single tear.

“Should we take him to a psychiatrist?” said mom.“If he looks up from his screen, he’d notice anything!” —dad.“He won’t let me watch baby shark! I want baby shark!”— Abhi’s 5-year-old sister.

While the entire household was having a meltdown around him, Abhi quietly played SPACECRAFT COMMANDERS— GALAXY INFINITY on his tablet. And didn’t look up once.

He was at his perfect next shot and a level-up when suddenly, somebody rudely confiscated his tablet. Abhi looked around, confused.“What did I do?”said his puzzled face to his angry dad.

“Listen up, Abhi.

No more tab for you, no more cellphones, no more TV, laptops and e-book readers. You will stay away from screens for the next month. And you will do as daddy says.”

“Bud daddy! — ”

“To your room! NOW!”

Abhi left the room bawling. His mother ran after him.

“You will get all your devices back in a few days baby. But now, you have to play with your toys and read those picture books aunty gave you”.

“But I don't want books! I don't want toys! I want to play SPACECRAFT COMMANDERS”.

“You will soon, baby. How about we go to Feebie and play with Aman and their dog? Don’t you miss Milo?”

At the mention of Milo Abhi’s bawling became quieter. He hugged his mother and buried his face in her chest, unsure about what he was feeling.

When Milo first came home last year, Abhi was delighted. They played together, rolled in the grass together, and showered together.

When daddy took Milo on walks, Abhi held the leash. Milo peed himself in happiness when Abhi came home from school.

But one evening, Aman came over on a playdate. He brought with him SPACECRAFT COMMANDERS — GALAXY INFINITY.

Abhi was mesmerized from the first touch. The graphics were so real! That feeling of sliding his finger on the gorilla glass was sosatisfying!

Abhi loved how the enemy spaceships exploded with a little “pop” when he hit them. With every upgrade, the bullets got thicker and the ships popped better. As he got faster, Abhi played more. And more. And more.

In the meanwhile, Milo perched in his dog house and watched Abhi, waiting for his turn.

Months passed. Dad fed and walked Milo alone and mom bathed and played with him. Abhi came home from school and rushed straight to SPACECRAFT COMMANDERS.

Every. Single. Day.

The next morning at Feebie’s, Abhi found Aman was on his bed, head buried in a screen. He heard the familiar Pew! Swoosh! Bang!

There it was — The beautiful SPACECRAFT COMMANDERS — GALAXY INFINITY. He approached Aman, fingers tingling for the sensation of a swipe. He looked back from his shoulder to see if his mother was watching. There she was at the door, smiling.

“Feebie wants to take Aman and the neighbors’ kids to the zoo in some time. Do you want to go?”

This was his chance to get some time alone with his game.“Yes, Ma. I want to go”.

Just as Abhi thought that Amma forgot about dad’s rules,she said to Feebie,“Thank you Feebie! please remember that he is not allowed around screens for a month. This is important”.

Abhi did not let his disappointment show.

Abhi got on the bus with Feebie and the rest of the kids. He was going to sneak a round of SPACECRAFT COMMANDERS when she wasn’t looking. As he waited, His mind wandered to Milo.Whatever happened to Milo? Where did mom and dad take him that morning? Why was he sleeping all the time?

Just then, he saw Milo on the street from the bus window!

He got up in a rush and hurried to get a closer look. He glued his face onto the milky-stained glass of the bus and turned his eyes as far to the right as they could go.Was that Milo?

The bus was fast, and Abhi sat down again. He’d pick up Milo on their way back, he was sure.

At the zoo, there were animals of all kinds. Abhi had a great time and forgot about his mission SPACECRAFT COMMANDER.

The day gradually drew to an end. Feebie and the kids had walked all day, and everyone was tired.

Together, they sat down under the giant red umbrellas at a cafe while Feebie lined up to get fries and ice cream for all.

Pew! Swoosh! Bang!There it was again — He could hear it but did not see where the sound came from. Abhi looked around, startled. Was it from behind that sunglass shop there? He was certain it was.

He jumped off his chair and followed the sound. It was not the sunglass shop.Was it from the sweet-corn parlor? Just one game.

Abhi walked around, hopping from one place to the next. But there was no SPACECRAFT COMMANDER. By now the sound had died out, leaving him nothing to follow. Abhi gave up, disappointed, but when he was ready to go back, he didn’t see Feebie and the kids anymore.

He looked at the many leaving buses and scanned intently for his group. There were parents, children, and groups of people everywhere. Abhi felt like he was the only one who was alone. Balloons shaped like animals floated in the air. He wondered who lost them. It smelled like evening snacks and cold mud. It was about to rain.

Abhi was scared. The sun had nearly set and the little stalls that sold ice cream and stuffed animals were closing. A huge crowd was emerging from the zoo, which was now also closed.

If only his dad let him keep his smartphone!

Did Feebie notice that he was missing? Abhi was red and tears started to emerge in his eyes.

Abhi did not know how to take the bus, or which one to take.How would he pick up Milo on the way?

He knew no phone numbers and could call no cab. He couldn’t remember his address, which he had marked on the maps app.

If only his dad let him keep his smartphone!

Angry at his father and still teary-eyed, Abhi walked on along a road that he thought would lead home. He wasn’t hungry, or thirsty, he just wanted to be home.

By now it was pitch dark.

The longer he walked, the more he realized that he was not going home.Where were all the buildings, all the closed shops, and the people on the road? Will he ever see mom again?These thoughts made him cry harder.

He sat down under what looked like a bus stop.Did buses even stop there?He hasn’t seen any in a long time. Abhi started to munch a piece of candy he found in his bag.

Just then, he heard a rustle in the bushes behind him. Abhi jumped, startled, and dropped his candy. His heart started to beat loudly.

Should he run?

Something hairy and large emerged from the bush.Was it a wolf? A dog?Abhi was terrified and wailed loudly. He could hear growls from this strange animal.

Abhi wanted to scream, “Ma!”, but no words came out from his dry throat.

He stood there, wailing and shivering, face-to-face with this strange creature, and at once, took off. He ran, and the wolf was after him.

All of a sudden, he heard a loud blow and cacophony behind him. Still terrified, he stopped and looked behind.

It was Milo! Gasp!

He had befallen the Wolf and wrestled him down. Milo howled in agony. Abhi still couldn’t see clearly in the darkness, but there they were, two hairy creatures rolling in the dust.

Abhi stood there, shell-shocked as the wolf ran away, and Milo with him. He screamed after them“Milo! Milo! Come back! Please come back!!”His wailing had become unstoppable. He fell down to a pile and cried.

Just then, in the dead silence of the night, came a screech. A car had spotted him and was pulling over. It was his dad’s car!

Abhi got up at once and ran towards the car even before his parents and Feebie alighted. His mother was in tears too. Both of them sobbed uncontrollably in each other's arms.

“Amma, Milo saved my life! He saved me!” wailed Abhi “Let’s take him home Amma!”

Mom and dad looked at each other.

“baby, Milo is safe where he is, and now you are too. Let us go home. I’m sure Milo will come when he wants to.”

Abhi was glad to be found. But the thought of leaving Milo behind broke his heart. It took his parents a long time to convince him and get him in the car.

After reaching home, dad gave Abhi all his devices back. But Abhi didn't rush to open SPACECRAFT COMMANDER. He hopefully went to Milos's dog house, crouched, and looked thoroughly.

Perhapshe followed us here too like he followed Abhi from the bus?

There was no Milo this time, but Milo would find him one day, he was sure. That day, Abhi would never let him go again. Not for any game in the world.

I am Sruthi Korlakunta. I write on tech, lifestyle, and books.

Read all you want on Medium by joining with my referral link here. It costs 5$ a month and totally worth it!

Thank youRama Mallavarapufor your ideas, exchange, and editing of this story!

title: Millions of Women aren’t “Just Exaggerating”
content:
Millions of Women aren’t “Just Exaggerating”

How patriotism kills the conversation

Like a broken gramophone record, I found myself in this conversation for the 100th time.

Me:I don’t feel safe back home. I can’t go out on a walk or even run an errand late in the evening.

Fellow patriotic Countryman:That doesn’t mean the country is unsafe. As many bad things could happen in developed nations as in ours.

Me:But they didn’t to me here! I was often bothered on the streets back home.

Him:It’s just the population. If we normalize the rape statistics to the population, even the most developed of countries look bad.

Me:But hey, at home, at work, in social circles, I feel less discriminated against here because of being a woman. Maybe there is a need to change something back home?

Him:WhereI come from, it is VERY safe for women.

Me:How can you tell? You are a man.

Him:I have a sister. Never heard her complain.

Me:I have a brother too. I never complained to him. You just don’t talk about it as if it’s a major event because it happens so often. The cultural subjugation of women is even accepted as “normal” in most households.

Him:You’re biased against your own people.

Me:It is not about me with or against my county, I want to feel safe. Is that too much to ask?

Him:We come from a great country. You shouldn’t talk ill about it among foreign nationals.

Me:…Right. Sorry…

This is how it always ends — Me apologizing for asking to feel safe in my country, or swallowing my conscience and saying “Right, I’m probably exaggerating”.

If I get any louder or elaborate I’m“that bi*ch”or worse still“feminazi”

Make no mistake; there is a problem in every corner of the world and not just in the developing nations. But all said and done, isn’t acknowledgment to what is wrong the first step to change? Or am I just delirious?

I try to put myself in the perspective of a man (As much as my minimal imagination allows me).

As a man in a rather orthodox country, Here is probably what the world looked like to me:

I was probably not witnessing to abuse in the form women seem to constantly complain of

I was raised, if not consciously, by the indirect language of the society as being the stronger sex of whom physical and mental strength is expected despite circumstances

Critique on physical appearances if any at all, are friendly banter or something I can make up for with my immense “capabilities”

I am the bearer of the family name and stand responsible for my parents in old age.

While women are a “liability” to be married off and educated, I am an “asset”.

There are obviously not the ideal conditions for a Man’s upbringing.This kind of upbringing obviously puts a large amount of pressure “to always perform” and “never vent”.

Together with the stereotype of men being a menace to women, whatever little fairness and empathy exist in men was systematical if not intentionally wiped out.

The result is the abominable#notallmen. Instead of diverting attention to the cause of making the world a better place, it has turned the world into a defensive sh*t storm that drops the discussion like it’s hot.

Adding fuel to the garbage fire is the concept of patriotism. What kind of patriotism leaves behind half the population in exchange for a “good-word-out-there”? If there is a pile of sh*t sitting in your living room, do you shush the talk about it while your friends come over, or do you clean it up?

If you are a man, the next time you deny that a problem exists, stop and listen. That already heals a large part of the wound. Passively nodding is more helpful than actively denying what you don’t know to be a lie.

Men aren’t from Mars and women aren’t from Venus. They pretty much all want to see, hear, and feel the same things.

Shocking? Yeah.. you’re welcome.

title: Take Your Career in Germany Into Your Own Hands
content:
Take Your Career in Germany Into Your Own Hands

Toastmasters Ingolstadt Welcomes Speakers with German as a Foreign Language

There is now an official German Language Public Speaking club in Ingolstadt. Why should you bother?

The “Why”

If you are working at a German firm, you know what I am talking about here. You made it through all that paperwork into a new country, you finished your Education from a reputed German university, you navigated all those embarrassing moments at the supermarket billing where you had no idea why the lady is talking to you in German instead of just taking your money and leaving you alone.

Congratulations, you are already half-way through to getting a decent job in Germany. But why stop there? Why do only the necessary and hope that a wonderful career will rain from the sky into your Outlook postfach.

Regardless of what level of proficiency you have in German Language, A1 to Z100, I am sure you have (I definitely have) fumbled your way through an extremely German meeting at work at one point or the other.

Was it the accent, was it the new technical terms, was it the extremely fast-speaking colleague or the “inside jokes” which you seem to always miss?

There might be times when you think “aaarg!, I know how to solve this problem” but couldn't make yourselfeffectivelyheard.

At this point, you have had enough classes of German. But for words to come out of your lips when they are most needed, you have to go the extra mile.

That is where Toastmasters comes in. Not only does it give you a stage, spotlight, and audience to shine in your moment but you get to do that while not being embarrassed about speaking ridiculous German.

What is the advantage over a German class?

You are surrounded by native proficient speakers and get to hear an earful of real German every 2 weeks for 2 hours.

You get to snatch the mic for yourself and give your personal best. You get to feel those butterflies in your tummy when you are talking German until you don't have them anymore.

What better reason can you have to join this club? there is only one such club in Ingolstadt ;)

I for one, am a speaker of German since 5 years, but still don't trust myself to write blog posts in German. My stage fright and speaking up in meetings has already gotten a lot better, but I'm still getting there ;). Meet people like myself and better at the Toastmasters club of Ingolstadt called the INgolSpeakers.

The “How”

If you don’t live in Ingolstadt, worry not. Go to Toastmasters.org and find your nearest German-speaking club. If you are in and around Ingolstadt, find them onfacebook, contact them throughthis formor if you prefer human contact first, find me onLinkedin.

The “When”

Yesterday. No, actually anywhere between Winter 2018 and this very moment would have been a good time to contact the Ingolspeakers. They meet every second and fourth Tuesdays of the month. Regarding venue and timings, just write them a line.

Hope I could motivate you to do something for your career today!

I am Sruthi Korlakunta, living in Ingolstadt since 2019. I work full-time for Audi as a Data Analyst.

If you moved here recently, Welcome to Ingolstadt. Feel free to get in touch onLinkedIn. If you want to be a part of Medium,join here.

If you are new to the city, check outhttps://ingolstadtexpats.com/for some great contacts in the city. It is a blog run by and for expats in the town and has some great content to get started

title: Ingolspeakers, Ihr Public Speaking Club im Herzen von Ingolstadt
content:
INgolSpeakers, Ihr Public Speaking Club im Herzen von Ingolstadt

Offizielle Toastmasters club von Ingolstadt

Wir, die Ingolspeakers - der offizielle deutschsprachige Toastmasters Club von Ingolstadt - freuen uns sehr, Sie auf unserer Seite begrüßen zu dürfen.

Seit 2018 sind wir ein äußerst enthusiastischer und schnell wachsender Verein und stolz darauf, Toastmasters der charmanten Stadt Ingolstadt näher gebracht zu haben.

Was ist Toastmasters?

Toastmasters International ist ein internationaler Redeclub mit Hauptsitz in den USA, die weltweit Clubs betreibt, um Kommunikation, Public Speaking und Führung zu fördern. Jeder der mindestens 18 Jahre alt ist, darf Toastmasters beitreten.

Bist du Anfänger? Hier bist du genau Richtig. Bist du eine Erfahrene Redner? Dann lernst du andere Erfahrene Rednern kennen ;) Die Hauptziele von Toastmasters sind:

Angst vor Öffentliches Reden Überwinden

Sich als Führungskraft Entwickeln

Team arbeit, Effizientes Plannen lernen

Spaß an Reden am Leben halten

Toastmasters International ist eine Internationale Rede Club um Kommunikation, Public Speaking und Führung zu fördern.

Hierlernst du mehr von das Toastmasters Konzept kennen.

Wer sind Ingolspeakers?

Wir sind 24 hoch motivierte Redner aus München und Ingolstadt. unser Alter ist zwischen 20 und 66 — und Rede Expertise ist alles zwischen Anfänger und Experte, mit den unterschiedlichsten Motivationen und Zielen.

unser Alter ist zwischen 20 und 66 — und Rede Expertise ist alles zwischen Anfänger und Experte

So sieht Ingolspeakers ungefähr aus:

Leute die sich in Führungspositionen entwickeln wollen

Junge Unternehmer

Frauen und Männer, die nach dem Auszug der Kinder neue Ziele suchen.

Ausländer die Ihre Deutsch verbessern wollen

Menschen die Nette Gesellschaft suchen

Frauen die sich Beruflich Neu-Orientieren

Profis die einfach Hervorragend reden

Anfänger, die Angst vor dem Reden haben

Wir sind natürlich aufFacebookaktiv, wo du unsere mitglieder persönlich Kennenlernen kannst. Oder du nutzt das Formular unten mit uns in Kontakt zu treten.

Ich bin begeistert, Wie komme ich rein?

Einfach! Nutz dieser TypeForm uns zu kontaktieren, und wir kommen auf dich zu.

Ich möchte für Ingolspeakers schreiben, wie geht das?

Wenn du einen Beitrag zur Medium-Seite von IngolSpeakers leisten möchtest, hinterlasse deine Medium-ID mit einem Schreibmuster, und wir werden dich als Autor hinzufügen.

Du kannst dann deine Arbeit zur Veröffentlichung auf der Seite Ingolspeakers Medium einreichen. Besseres Schreiben sorgt für besseres Sprechen, also los!

title: 4 Ways to Find the Elusive Meaning To Life
content:
4 Ways to Find the Elusive Meaning To Life

And what to do when you can’t.

The most interesting moments in life are those of transition. Working resolutions, changes, places, new hobbies, new love — These things keep you alive, relevant, important, andMeaningful.

What happens when you settle in and wait? What happens when things are not going your way and you are just happy enough to not want more? You feel empty and meaningless. You feel stuck and hopeless.

Little acts of kindness, social activity, and vacations may help you sweep your feelings under the carpet and keep going, but there are better ways of dealing with thefeeling ofpurposeconsistently. What do you do in these phases?

1. Find a project and don't ask why.

Take that course you always wanted to do, (it is probably now available online!), prepare for that marathon, and plan your cross-country bike ride. Walk for a thousand miles like Forrest Gump. Go on all those dates and see if one of them fits.

Most importantly, don’t ask why. Feel powerful about having picked something up completely out of your free will, even if it has no consequence and utility. Do it because of your curiosity.

2. Find a moral high ground and don't talk about it.

Take in a refugee. Plant a tree. Volunteer. Donate. Heal an animal. Be kind to that asshole you always hated. Don’t explain to anyone, and don’t post on social.

Take in the moral high ground, it's okay to feel good about good deeds. You will get validation of authenticity if you don’t talk about it. If you do, you might think that you are doing it only for praise.

Get the full benefit of meaning by keeping it quiet.

3. Live outside your head.

Say things out loud.

Find someone you can trust and trust them with your vulnerabilities. Tell them how much it means that they are around. It can be risky but rewarding. It is not just a privilege you allow yourself but also something you allow the listener.

Your friends are not your therapists. So when you ask for an ear, also lend one. It will help both of you look at your lives from a third-person perspective.

4. Accept that your days are not a road to go “somewhere”

Your job isn't going anywhere, your love life is dead and friends don’t even see you every week. What now? Stop running from it all.

There is a tendency to imagine life as a raw piece of clay and your goal is to mold it into some shape. Sometimes, it is just fun to dig your fingers in and enjoy the mud. Try not to “go somewhere else”, “get something else”. The harder you run, the more you feel stuck.

Stay and luxuriate in the choice you made to stay. Your today is as important as the day you are molding your time into.

If I convinced you to become a member on medium,join here, I’d love to see you and read from you!

Here is my social, I amSruthi Korlakunta.

In my day job, I am a Data Analyst for Audi. During and after work, I vigorously explore the world and my mind for the truth.

title: Leading a Data Science Project from Scratch
content:
Leading a Data Science Project from Scratch

Every data science project, ever.

If you are new to leading a project in data science, you will have many questions despite having gone through the same steps one too many times as an intern, or an engineer in the team.

When it comes to leading a project, you need a bird’s eye view into what makes your project a good product. Something your clients will kill or die for.

In order to give you a blueprint for the organization of your upcoming project lead role, here are somethings that are common to every data science project from the scratch.

1. Idea.

First and foremost, set up a portfolio of projects that can be put to life. This part is slightly more difficult than it looks on the outside because project ideas can come from a bazillion places.

It is impossible to foresee all the roadblocks in advance and kick off a project with certainty that it works. Start with accepting this uncertainty and kicking off a project where the need is pressing.

The following questions will very well help you to zero-in on your POI.

What your clients have been screaming for since forever.

What your client can benefit from, but doesn't know it yet.

Where have there been escalations because of missing information?

Where is readily available data but hiding in the dark?

Which one blindspot, when removed, will cause enlightenment in 10 other areas.

These are usually the areas that have juicy projects.

Remember that projects don’t always have to put Teslas in space. Sometimes, uncovering one previously unknown column can cause tears of joy.

Respect the tiny wins. They often lead to bigger wins.

2. Data collection

Get your hands dirty. Real-world data is not Kaggle-clean. Spend enough time, identify all sources of information.

Check if you need additional hardware. Real data collection can take anything from a month to a couple of years depending on the complexity of your project and availability of records. Some data might be manually documented (tickets, reviews, etc.). Don't ignore these sources.

In Machine learning or artificial intelligence projects, An additional plus is when you can augment your collected data with artificial information. See if you can use open-source images for your need, generate your own datasets, manipulate your existing data and augment the datasets with more information.

3. Data Cleaning

This step is the pandora’s box of data processing. Open it.

Here are some thingsyou should think of while you clean your data. Use this to weed the unwanted information in your datasets and make sure that you are looking at what you think you are looking at.

After cleaning your dataset, usable data might be much slimmer than the original data set. Be prepared to reframe your question in ways that you can with the clean data you have.

An important aspect of data cleaning is people. Huge datasets and computation power are a thing of the past couple of decades but people have been dealing with systems and data for centuries.

Meet with everyone in peace and understand what their role in the system is. Get all the valuable information you can and augment your knowledge of the system.

4. Exploratory Data analysis

Produce summaries of your data that can be immediately understood by your clients. Condense, analyze and interpret the meaning, and discuss your insights with the concerned parties.

Understand the relationships between your parameters, remove faulty records, anomalies, and get insights at every level of analysis. Differentiate between categorical and continuous data and deal with them accordingly.

Depending on whether your data is categorical or continuous, you will choose a method to build your model. Check for central tendencies and variabilities (mean, median, mode, standard deviation, range, etc.). Summarize and visualize your data. Identify frequencies, seasonalities, percentages, and the like.

You might be amused to know that many experts might be surprised at the most basic findings you have on visualizing and condensing the data.

5. Model building

After selecting your data and features, understand the basic nature of what you are dealing with, you will come to the point of creating a model.

Do you want to perform regression, classification, clustering, or forecasting? how much data you have? What the nature of it (categorical, continuous)? Depending on your answers to these questions, you will choose a model. Actually, you might select multiple models to start with.

Pay attention to whether your data is linearly separable, what your target variables are, etc. You might choose to perform Linear regression, logistic regression, clustering models such as Random forests, K-means, neural networks etc.

Depending on your model metrics, your computation power, and the need for prediction, or insight, choose one model out of your experiments. Then, validate your model with more data or data you freshly collected since your data collection process ended.

If you are using machine learning, see that your results generalize.

6. Production

Once you found the correct model, set of hyperparameters and you are sure your model works, you will want to deploy it.

While you deploy your project, you should think about how it scales and how you can make the user interface friendly to your client.

To deploy your model and make it widely usable, you might want to create a web application to which you can send and receive requests. Else, you might save your model and let your users use it to get predictions for their data.

You can implement your model in web application frameworks such as Django, flask, shiny, or dash. For scalability, many big firms use services such as AWS or Azure to deploy machine learning models on a large scale.

That should be a quick brush into starting your next big data science project. Do you do it differently?

title: Tinder in a Small German Town
content:
Tinder in a Small German Town

Online dating in Ingolstadt

Before the pandemic, I was ever on a dating app for about a whole hour. More out of curiosity and as a joke. My first time on the app was 5 years before that, as a student, hardly with an intention tofind someone. I wanted to have been there, done that.

The“real matches” of my life, I had always met offline. Romance used to be a wildflower. It bloomed out of the blue without having to tend to and water it. If I was at the beginning of “something sweet”, I always knew within the first few moments. And I was always right. One glance and a conversation were all it took.

Thanks to the lockdown, home office, and the general death of enthusiasm during cold-weather, dating apps have become nearly the only way to meet new people with an intent to keep seeing them. Ergo, I landed on tinder. In Ingolstadt.

Dating in small German Towns

In a small German town like the one I live in, running into the right people happens only if you force it. Germans are calm and mostly invisible unless you find a hobby club to go to. Even if you do, encounters with strangers feel rehearsed and scripted —Where are you from, what’s the weather in the south of India like, do you like spicy food?

Connectionbegins when the script ends. So I decided to go off-script. I found it easier to meet cultures that are more naturally given to talking than the German culture. Not a great many people if you rule out the entire local population.

Add to it my woes of having grown up in India, where formally dating wasn’t even a thing in my city until I decided to leave the country. Oh boy.

Location Settings

If it is one thing to live and date in a small town, it makes matters worse that the town is located an hour away from the next big city — Munich. The idea of having to travel an hour for a date seems agreeable only until the first date. When it is finally your turn to drive, you tell yourself“Aren’t there men in Ingolstadt” —back to location settingsin a radius of 3 miles. (Perhaps the breadth of Ingolstadt).

After you have seen all your co-workers, all your friend’s boyfriends(for what reason ever, I do not know),all people from your own country who ended up in the same small town and on the app, you decide to finally take your next date seriously and not find excuses to dislike the person. You are running out of kind and eligible men in the toy town.

The first date

Without pre-conceived approval of the pheromones by your brain, without laughing at a joke not intended to impress, and without having a chance to observe the stranger in his “natural habitat”, in online dating, everything rests on that first date.

You go in looking for that “X-factor” which makes you feel more feminine around this particular boy than others. It’s hard to pinpoint and explain, but you look anyway, usually in vain.

The thing with small towns like Ingolstadt is, if you take one step into the busy part of town where all of the bars are located, you run into everyone you know. Not a great setting to go on dates you might later want to forget about.

The awkwardness of running into a non-exclusive-yet-active date while you are on another non-exclusive-yet-active date has no parallel in shame. You would feel like a jerk without really doing anything wrong.

The trickiest part is staying interested when it is not your first first-date anymore. You are sick of introducing yourself. You love painting, writing, drawing, biking but you are sick of talking about them all. You don't want to be liked for your hobbies, you don't want to use them as what makes you “saleable material” on the dating market. They are yours for you. You also don’t want to talk about your job to look like“ a dependable and smart woman”.It is too materialistic a ground for love.

So what do you talk about and what do youlistenabout?

If you successfully navigated the awkwardness of the first couple of dates, then comes the question at around date 5 — What are the rules?

At what point do you even develop feelings? At what point should I really stop letting him pay? When do you stop seeing other people? Do you talk about it? How soon is too soon to go away for the weekend? Should I cut my losses or is this really going somewhere?

Finding love is like sayingtry not to think about a blue elephant.You cannot force it by forcing it.

Somehow, your body has buttons and you have no idea what they might be. They light up when they want, it could be for a Hobo, a nerd, or a normal-o like yourself.

In real life, you might fall for someone you wouldn't even spend a millisecond on a dating app. On an app, you might end up getting lunch with someone who has nothing in common with you because both of you like cheese crackers. Online dating is a strange place. However it needs to be navigated, I have not figured it out yet.

If you reject the traditional Indian method of letting your parents find you the right boy, in a town like Ingolstadt, you really are at the mercy of time. Let us see how far the algorithms bring us.

I am Sruthi Korlakunta. I work full-time for Audi as a Data Analyst. Read all you want on Medium from me and others by joining with my referral link. It costs 5$ a month and it changed my life!

If you have a story to share, hit me up onLinkedIn! I will give your story the voice it deserves.

If you are new to the city, check outhttps://ingolstadtexpats.com/for some great contacts in the city. It is a blog run by and for expats in the town and has some great content to get started.

title: Narcs of Netflix
content:
Narcs of Netflix

No-Blood True Crime Documentaries

I hate gore, but I’m interested in the intellectual autopsy of crimes. You don’t need blood to be a monster, anyone who has lived with a Narcissist can confirm. Here is a curated list of the most jaw-dropping true crime series on Netflix that feature zero violence, yet represent the absolute peak of human depravity. “The God Complex” in action.

#1 Bad Vegan

(Small spoilers here.)

My jaw was on the ground floor the whole time. The story is just so insanely implausible. To believe this story, you must have been a victim of or have borne witness to any form of emotional abuse or manipulation. To anyone else, it’s batshit.

A strikingly beautiful and unusually successful woman, Sarma Melngailis (once coveted by Alec Baldwin), believes that a small-time crook can make her and her dog immortal, siphons hundreds of millions of dollars to him, while he uses the story of Thor as if it were his own, and he were Thor.

And marries him. (!!!!!)

Bad Vegan is a documentary narrated by the victim firsthand before she goes away to prison, while her abuser, who actually benefited from the Fraud walks free.

#2 Madoff: The Monster of Wall Street

The story of Bernie Madoff is the ultimate case study in compounded absurdity. To look back at the world’s largest financial scam is to witness a theater of idiots: Madoff himself, the clients who wanted risk-free returns, and the regulators who ignored mathematical proof of his fraud for decades.

The series charts Madoff’s ascent from a Wall Street hero and NASDAQ pioneer to the architect of a “cheap” Ponzi scheme. Unfortunately, the documentary only commits to the story but not the man behind the math. I felt like it could have benefitted from more psych-evaluations of Madoff himself.

What psyche is required to sustain a failing mission for forty years without ever seeking an exit strategy? What level of pathological narcissism does it take to knowingly defraud everyone, from American billionaires to European royalty? While the series captures thehowof the fraud, thewhyof Madoff’s internal engine remains a chilling mystery.

Despite that missing piece of the puzzle, this chronicle of history’s most significant financial betrayal is absolute, essential viewing.

#3 Woodstock 99

An episode from the Trainwreck series, Woodstock 99, shows the unfolding of the Woodstock music festival in New York in 1999. The documentary is a series of narrations firsthand from the organizers, interns, and attendees of the festival.

In an attempt to make as much money out of it as possible, Woodstock was understaffed and under-resourced, leaving raging topless adolescents on drugs in scorching heat and enraging music. The attendees, angered by the inhuman treatment, form a zombie-like mob and tear down the festival and set it on fire by the end of it all (by candles provided by the management, nonetheless).

The most interesting part of this episode is that, despite the nightmarish organization, everyone on Reddit who has been to this festival said they’d 100% go again.

#4 Bad Surgeon

Bad Surgeonis a horrifying look at how a god complex in a narcissist can escalate to a death sentence. Paolo Macchiarini used medical institutions as his personal laboratory to perform his own WW-II style absurd experiments all while being hailed as a visionary “super-surgeon”. It’s infuriating to watch how easily the medical elite — and even a seasoned NBC producer — fell for his fabricated charisma.

It raises the same haunting question as the Madoff scandal: how much “proof” do people need before they stop choosing a beautiful lie over a dangerous truth?

The documentary excels at showing the visceral cost of his lies, but it leaves you reeling at the lack of accountability. How does a man perform experimental surgery on human beings with zero successful animal trials and no oversight? A deeply unsettling, mandatory watch for anyone interested in the dark side of innovation.

#5 Apple Cider Vinegar

There is something about fraud influencers that is beyond cringe. Something along the lines of visceral revulsion or moral disgust. This isn’t just an awkward reaction faux pas, but something very primal that tells you you are in the presence of a predator.

Belle Gibson’s story falls under this category. Gibson sold the ultimate lie, that Stage IV brain cancer could be cured with a lifestyle, a glow, and a bottle of apple cider vinegar.

The series is an autopsy ofmalignant sympathy.Gibson built a global empire, convincing vulnerable people to trade their chemotherapy for açai bowls.

What makes this “stupidity” so tragic compared to Madoff is the victim pool. Madoff’s victims were blinded by greed; Gibson’s victims were blinded by a survival instinct. It takes a specific, hollowed-out brand of narcissism to watch your “followers” literally die while following your fake advice, all while you maintain the curated facade of a wellness guru.

If you enjoyed this article, maybe you will also enjoy this one aboutSpectacular Non-violent Crimes on Netflix.

title: Start
content:
Start

Plan: 70,000 words.

Daily life: 7000 words

Call to adventure and acceptance: 7000 words (cum 14k words)

challenges and action: 21000 words, cumulative: 35000

death and resurrection: 30,000 words ( cum 65000)

atonement and return: 5000 words.

Modern elements: Lesbianism,

End of sixth semester

“He wanted to talk about his start-up. To see if I can help somehow”.

I lied casually and mom knows. She suspects that something is cooking between Harsh and me but she would never confront me. After all these years of marriage, it doesn’t occur to her that dad is only human, we could do things our way, if only in secret.

As long as she is afraid of drawing attention,I can be careless around her.

If we were to choose a title for our family as if it were a movie, it would beSweep it under the rug.We don’t talk or listen. We just do as we are told. Our way to deal with emotions is to pretend that we don’t have any.

“Shh!”,said mother, flipping chapatis.“ Not so loud, Your father might hear. What did you do? Were there also other people? other girls?”

“Ramya and Bhuvi were there too. He has a great business Idea, I can work for him after college”.I lied again.

I wonder why we even try to keep up the charade — mom and me. We both know we aren’t going to talk about love, relationships, or, god forbid, the S-word. Because obviously, a girl from a “traditional family” like ours will let let her parents find the ultimate man for her, and be a good wife until her children come along. In reality, I had just spent the day making out with Harsh at the movies.

“Anju!”dad hollered from the living room.“Usha attha is here!”

I wasn’t in the mood to see UshaAttha(Telugu for auntie). She has been pestering my father to marry me off before it istoo late. Everybody was worried that I am gaining weight and aging at a pace they didn’t expect. I was already 19. They want me gone, preferably into a rich family.

Nobody asked me if I wanted that. They were only worried I’d mouth off to my potential grooms, win us a bad reputation, and end up never being married.

“I have to watch the stove nanna (Dad), can't come!”I shouted back, taking over the chapati-flipping. This was my excuse to stay in the kitchen, and my form of toothless rebellion.

My family lives in a poor zip code, in a house that looked misplaced in among the match-box-like one-room families around us. An odd apartment sprung here and there over decades where the families of the newly-middle-class lived. Among these was my modest two-bedroom home with a front yard that my father built over many years.

18 years ago, my parents moved to Hyderabad from a village about a thousand miles away. I was born soon after. The world my parents were prepared for was different from the one it actually turned out to be. We grew up listening to the cricket match on the radio, booking telephone calls in advance, watching scheduled programs on the TV and waiting for the news paper to know the world. The change must have been all the more dramatic to my parents.

By the time she was married, things had changed drastically for my mother. As far as I can remember, she hadn’t had an opinion of her own.“Ask your dad”was her response to everything.

My parents somehow managed to build our lives up from zero. Dad was a clerk at the Local Public Transport Authority and mom gave up her teaching job to raise us.

I have an older sister — Nidhi. She moved to the USA with her husband after marriage, and did not resist when she had to end her secret relationship with her boyfriend of 5 years for the wedding. I, on the other hand, was a disappointment  from the first day. They’d hoped I’d be a boy.

Before his retirement, I was the only remaining responsibility on his to-do list. He was determined to make “Project Anju” a success and keep his reputation untarnished.

This also means a whole set of stringent rules for me. I was to be home right after college, not to meet friends outside of my home, and not to have any boys call me or talk to me when I wasn’t accompanied. I was always to dress in loosely fitted clothes with adupattaover my breasts. The very fact that I was developing breasts was revolting to my father, as was his interference in my dressing to me.

At dinner, we ate quietly while watching TV. This was our daily ritual.

“Durex, now ribbed — Get it on!”,an awkwardly placed condom commercial in the middle of a family dinner.Oops!

Dad changed the channel without an expression on his face.“Sweep it under the rug”.The evening news was always acceptable.

“26-year-old girl found dead in an abandoned parking lot in Nagole, suspects arrested.”

Love was taboo but murder was not. He let it be.

Boys in my city have the privilege to leave and enter the home as they please, but girls were always under surveillance. One hour after college, if I wasn’t home my phone would be abuzz with a screaming dad on the other side.

The constant bad news on TV about abduction and rape doesn’t help at all. It terrifies my already terrified parents and tightens the noose around my freedom.

After dinner, I finally retreated to my room — my favorite part of the day.

I lay in bed and tried to think about Harsh and the upcoming job interviews at college. Every year, some big names in the IT industry come to our famously mediocre engineering college and pick the top of the most mediocre of students for their esteemed organization.

If I got that job, it would change everything for me. I’d finally be free.

What about Harsh?

I try to fall in love with Harsh again, my boyfriend of a year.I try.But that love that came easy at the beginning didn’t show up in a long time.

When I decided to say yes to being his girlfriend, I was trying to score a win against my parents. I wanted to be better than Nidhi, who met her husband a grand total of twice before marrying him. She wasn’t ready. My parentsswept it under the rug.

Harsh did nothing wrong to make me fall out of love. His tastes and distastes were different from mine.

Heobviouslywanted to get a decent local job,obviouslymove into a bigger house with his parents forever,obviouslywatch the latest Bollywood hits let life never change too much.

I wanted out of the only city I ever knew. I wanted to see other parts of India, and who knows, maybe even the world. Maybe one day, I can even afford to travel only for fun!

I wanted this relationship to be over but never found the courage. OrMaybe I can just sweep this under the rug and marry him.

Faiza, Bhuvi and I, at the College Cafetaria

Faiza, Bhuvi, and I sat there waiting for the results to see if we got the job. The three of us met on our first day at college and stuck together ever since.

Faiza lived in my neighborhood. Her mom ran a mini-department store and her dad was a cook at a restaurant nearby. She needed a job more than any of us. Finally, she would contribute to running her family.

350 students had taken part in the interviews that day. About one-third of us would make it. ThatThirty-thousand-rupees-a-monthjob would be my ticket out of Hyderabad. Maybe to Bangalore.Hopefully to Bangalore.

It isn't a lot of money, but  enough to begin with. We could share an apartment with two others and afford it.

The three of us chatted and waited all day, with our fingers crossed. In the middle of the din and mayhem of who made it through to a job and who did not, the college campus was a mix of adrenalin and tears. The results were out.

Faiza and I made it! We could both go to Bangalore!

Bhuvi did not.“I am not allowed to work anyway, my in-laws won’t like it”— she wasn’t delighted, but she let it be.

Dad, at home

I was overjoyed. I went home expecting to finally meet my dad at eye level as a soon-to-be-earning member of the family.

For me, it meant no more asking dad to pay my fees. No more counting pennies at the cafeteria.My dad would be so happy.I eagerly waited for him to come home. Once he settled down on the couch with a magazine, I gave him the news.

“Oh! Congratulations Anju, that’s wonderful. But don’t get your hopes up, you’re not going anywhere.”

“But why?”— I managed after swallowing the knot in my throat.“It is a great job. hundreds of students wanted it, and they chose me!”

Usually, when my dad asks something of us, it already has been decided. My mother and I were at end of that process, with no role in decision-making. Any argument was going to be futile.That is why we just sweep things under the rug.

“Usha attha was here again.”He said with hardly any emotion.“She has a great match for you. You will marry him and then choose with your new family what you can and cannot do.”

I couldn’t believe my ears. From dad to husband, the ownership would only change.

“But nanna I want to go to Bangalore.”

“Anju, you have no idea what you want. Please don’t be difficult and ruin this for all of us. Can you now please go and help your mother with the dishes?”

My phone buzzed. It was Harsh. I was in no mood.

I stormed off inside.“No! I won't”I shouted from my room after locking it, not having to make eye contact with my dad. I was red and teary, but I was proud of having said No to something at least.

It didn’t matter to my dad that I had flesh and blood. I was his puppet to move as he pleases. He was surprised at any activity I had and any opinion I expressed as if he just discovered that I have life.

He was also quick to snub it, letting me know that I am the cause of his troubles, or that my mother has been a bad mother for letting me become the way I turned out.

I have heard it all, nothing was about to change this particular night. It was no use trying. I cried myself to sleep.

It has been a month since the debacle. It was never addressed.Who is this man? What was he like?I had no idea.

“Tomorrow, they are coming to meet you. pick out decent clothes and your mother’s jewelry.”He said dryly.

I remember Nidhi on the evening before her wedding. I heard dad’s loud and angry voice from behind the closed doors. I was only 9 but not too young to understand that she didn’t want it.

That evening, I sat on the terrace and played out the possibilities I had in my head.

What if I force myself free? Kick and scream my way out of this?What if I fail after all that? what if I cannot do it? Will my parents ever have my back? Will I be disowned and ostracized?

On the other hand, what if I marry this man and hate him? Divorce was unthinkable. This is going to be a one-way street. What if I actually like him? What are the odds that that ever happens?

There was no winning in this game. It was rigged against me from the beginning.

At a little before 2 AM, I snuck out.

TK

Harsh, at the mall

Maybe it was a small win I wanted, maybe I was just exhausted from all the not-fighting. I ripped off the band-aid.

“But we’re perfect! we love each other! I’ll talk to your dad, I’ll convince him, I have a great job too!”

My stomach turned at the mention of my dad. I am not a prize to be convinced.“Why isn’t anybody thinking of what I want?!”

“I don’t want to, Harsh. I dont want you to convince my dad, I don’t want to move into a home with you, I don’t want to be your family. I don’t know what I want, but it is not you, please let this go.”

I felt terrible looking at his teary face. I wish he’d stop crying. Everyone at the mall was staring at us. I felt like I was evil.

Isn't it human and normal to be attracted to boys when you are 20? and then to realize that it was a bad idea?

“I will try again, you don’t mean it, you just need time, you’ll remember all the great times we had, and you will want to come back to me.”

“No! This is it. I want you to stop trying.”I said the words I imagined forever, but still wasn’t certain they were right. Will I regret this later?How will I feel when I see him with another woman?I pictured it and felt —relieved.

“Bye Harsh, please stop calling me.”

He crumbled to the floor. “Please tell me this is a lie. I did everything right, I loved you so much. No one will ever love you like I did. You won’t find a better guy, you are making a mistake.”

“Bye. I am leaving now.”

I could hear him bawling behind me. More eyes on me. I walked faster.

Faiza, on the terrace

By the time I turned my phone back on at home, Harsh had tried to call me Eighty two times and left sixty three messages. I opened three of them and deleted the rest. I couldn’t stand it anymore.

I asked Faiza to meet me at home. We found our refuge on the terrace, where my parents wouldn’t find us. Faiza had already made arrangements to move to Bangalore after our final exams.

Neither were highly educated, but somehow, learnt that their only girl-child was human too.“How lucky!”

I told her about Harsh, and about the new boy my dad brought into the picture. She thought it wasn’t a terrible idea to be married.

May be the new guy will let you be free?

How is it freedom if someone has to give it to you?

Faiza sees life more practically than I do. She is a product of her parent’s hard work and life-lessons learned from  poverty. To be financially safe was her utmost priority. Marriage was a transaction to be made if it had decent returns.

I guess I have the luxury of romaticizing life too much. I want to keep doing it. Why should I succumb if I don’thave to?

title: The 5 Best Types of People on the Planet
content:
The 5 Best Types of People on the Planet

Thank god for them.

Empathy is biologically built into you. What another person feels, you cannot help but feel. If you are around happy and positive people, chances are that you will be one, thanks to your mirror neurons.

If there is one skill to learn before you step into your 30s, it is probably to find people who will help you keep your peace and to grow, and surround yourself with them.

If you see one or more of these qualities in people around you, don’t let them go, and hope that some of it rubs off on you too!

1. The givers

Some people are all about taking. They demand with entitlement and give only begrudgingly. Even while they take, they tell you how little it matters that you help.

On the other hand, are the givers, they open your doors to you, they reciprocate your acts and favors, and remember what you do for them, no matter how small it is.

Be a giver and surround yourself with givers.

2. The meta-thinkers

We are all human, we all do things we are not proud of. Self-awareness makes us aware of our own good and bad.

People with self-awareness sooner or later work on becoming better people. They have restraint for their own jealousy, their own pettiness, and their own misgivings.

They might also have a tolerance for your minor flaws and won't hold it against you if you once happen to be humanly imperfect.

On the other hand, there are those who will remember your flaws forever and narrate them at every gathering. Stay away.

3. The Meet-you-at-eye-level-ers

We all come from different cultures, different types of families, values, and morals. Some of us have learned that we are all in a way, similar. We all want the same — respect, love, consideration, and empathy.

These people are capable of meeting others at eye level. Whether they are blue-collar or white-collar, adult or adolescent, eastern or western hemisphere. They don’t look down at you even if they are in a conventionally higher rung on the ladder.

It is a delight to talk to people who can meet you at eye level without evaluating if they may be condescending to you. If you meet them, don’t let go.

4. The Cheerleaders

I know some people who, no matter how little I see them, never fail to leave a good note on something they liked to read or some wish on a special day.

Given that I myself could not always return the favor, they look like angels to me.How can someone be so supportive?!

If you spot a cheerleader, give them a cheer back. They are a rare species in the dog-eat-dog world. They empathize and they love you back.

Note, like-for-like and follow-for-follow do not count at all, for obvious reasons.

5. The idea machines

Some people fill me with wonder — Constantly experimenting, discovering new avenues, technologies, skills, hobbies, and roads to travel.

They are a great company to keep. People full of ideas, projects, and innovation are great sources of inspiration.

Life is an endless drama of figuring out everything new and positive, and then at some point, letting go. The idea machines make the process a joy. If you find an idea machine, latch on and rub some ideas off on yourself!

title: Writing Lessons From Leo Tolstoy
content:
Writing Lessons From Leo Tolstoy

In awe of Anna Karenina

“If the world could write itself, it would write like Tolstoy". How true! The past few weeks, I read Tolstoy’s Anna Karenina. I am otherwise not a fan of fiction and prefer books of nonfiction. but this book really hits the spot for anyone looking for realism in literature or wants to learn a thing or two. No wonder it has been hailed as “the greatest novel ever written” by many critics and Tolstoy himself considers this his first true Novel.

It is hard to not think about the‘making of’of such a book as this. As I read it, it felt like I was watching a painting being made. Tolstoy has a way of laying an outline, filling in the details, and creating an atmosphere with adjectives, as if creating emotions with strokes in a painting.

Like every painter has a style and favorite subject, his favorite subject appears to be the “realness” of being human. This is in accordance with Tolstoy’s widely known belief that theNovelis a social educator, not just means of entertainment. It serves to give the society a nuanced view of the psyche of a person in socially taboo situations thus leading to extending the boundaries of the reader’s empathy.

The most striking aspect of his work is how he plunges into the mind of his character and describes exactly what, who and why he is. This article explores Tolstoyianextreme-humanizingof stories.

Tolstoy’s word-painting

The outline of Anna Karenina

In the book Anna Karenina, all characters are oddly likable, each while still being in adiscomfort zoneof the society. There are no villains, but there are many imperfect people. There is hardly a character you want to root for without ambiguity.

This is the general outline of every character in the book. As if he decided at the outset:There are no demons or heros, only objectivity. Togo back to his philosophy, Tolstoy uses this novel to ‘un-taboo’ and humanize. It extends the range of acceptance and judgment through reason and empathy.

For example, Anna is a “perfect” society woman - full of love and empathy, a good friend, wife, and mother. She falls passionately in love outside of her marriage with Vronsky, a passion which she tries to resist with all her strength, but fails. She is excited by everything life has to offer, but remains trapped in the walls of a pious and unforgiving marriage and a similar society.

This, is a very relatable character, albeit in a situation you would not wish upon yourself or condone in another. By making a controversial moral character the protagonist and keeping us on her side, Tolstoy achieves the feat of teaching empathy to the most dogmatic reader.

The backdrop for Tolstoy’s word painting

When Tolstoy introduces a character, he describes in total their social and political affiliations, beliefs and motivations as he works into the story and characters. This information may not be directly relevant to the story but makes the backdrop of the kind of person in his day and age very concrete. It throws light on the reader’s own motivations while he least suspects it. For example, talking about Oblonsky, Tolstoy describes:

“Stephan Arkadyevitch read the liberal newspaper. Not an extreme one, but the one advocating the views of the majority. Inspite of not being interested in science, art or politics he held the views which were held by the majority and changed them when the majority change them. Or more strictly speaking, he did not change them, but they changed themselves within him. Stephan Arkadyevitch has not chosen his opinions or views, just like he did not choose the shapes of his coat and hat but took the ones that were already being worn”.

The significance of detail in painting such a backdrop is that it completes in our head the image of the kind of person we are dealing with. He is already an “anchor” for empathy for the reader. The description does not invoke explicit respect or disrespect for the person being talked about but calls to mind a relatable character from the current-day life of the reader.

This kind of political and moral backdrop to the novel also makes it timeless. In about a dozen places all over the book, I was flabbergasted to find parallels between today and the 1800s, how life has changed so little.

Filling in the details

Tolstoy takes time. He stays with the character until he or she feels completely familiar to us, until weknowwhence their choices and actions come. We see the character at home, at work, we see what they aspire to be, what fills their head, how they dress, and who they like. The level of detail in describing their demeanor, body language, facial expressions, dressing, and expectation is uncanny. He talks about minutia like the gait, class, expression, tone, simplicity, or extravagance of every character who walks into the scene.

Physical appearances are well written, nevertheless what sets apart his work is his description of people’s reactions to situations and what goes on in a character’s head while he makes a choice. Although this might seem a very obvious thing to do at first sight, in the book, this is done meticulously, leaving no reaction or thought process unexplained. Every time a character goes through the smallest introspection, this is made visible to the reader in all its specificity. For example, here is the description of Anna’s husband who just started to speculate if his wife was cheating on him, and was setting up a confrontation:

“All his life, Alexey Alexandrovitch had lived and worked in official spheres, (only) dealing with the reflection of life. And everytime he stumbled upon life itself, he shrunk away from it. Now he experienced a feeling akin to a man, who, while crossing the precipice of a bridge discovers that the bridge is broken and there is a chasm below. The chasm was life itself, the bridge the artificial life that Alexey Alexandrovitch has lived.”

The intense imagery of the metaphor, this one, and many others is unparalleled in any other works I have read.

To every situation which calls for the application of “self, moral and values” Tolstoy takes the time and word count to explain. Here is another example, of Anna Karenina reacting to her husband’s confrontation:

“Anna said what came to her lips, and marveled, hearing herself at her own capacity for lying. How simple and natural her words were, how likely that she was simply sleepy! She felt herself clad in an impenetrable armor of falsehood. She felt like some unseen force has come to her aid and was supporting her.”

Why it works

When watching a person act or react, we as audiences make many assumptions. Each of our assumptions as to the motivation of action or dialogue are different. They are different from each other and also from that of the author. The moment the author makes the reasoning clear, doubt and judgment fade and give place to empathy and relatability. Since most people are logical beings, the reasons also for unsavory reactions are understandable if explained. Movies and other forms of communication do not have an affordance to accurately depict motivation and internal logic, but books do. Tolstoy makes use of this affordance to its most potent level in Anna Karenina.

title: A Python Hack to Half Your Work: Automate Your Execution
content:
A Python Hack to Half Your Work: Automate Your Execution

Automate script execution and email the results with VBA, Powershell, and Task scheduler

Abstract:This article covers how to schedule your regular python scripts. Assuming that you

Get updated excel reports every day/week/month into a shared Network or email

Have a python script that is up and running to processes your reports and deliver insights in the form of images, worksheets, or other forms of data.

Want to run the process over an extended period at regular intervals of time but heavily dread having to run it manually.

There are fun parts of Data Engineering — like programming and analyzing data. Then there are boring parts, like using the already written programs on a regular basis to get the most recent results. Clearly, nobody wants to do the latter. Here is a quick fix I use to avoid running those scripts which need to be executed daily/weekly/monthly so that this time can be put to use better.

Three words — Macro, Batch, and schedule.

I use amacro to savemy incoming excel reports to a location as and when they come. (I get it from a place which performs the analysis and sends a report to me manually. No, there is no way around this).

I set up anoutlook ruleto run the macro only on desired folders along with few other conditions (sender, subject etc).

Iwrite a batch file(.bat), which on clicking executes the script on command prompt.

I schedule the bat file using theWindows Task schedulerto execute the script with the same frequency as the arrival of new excel reports

I write aPowerShell scriptto email the newest results to all the necessary recipients and then I schedule it to run soon after the bat file executes.

Step 1: Outlook Macros

You can activate VBA scripting in Outlook by activating it in the options pane of outlook.

You will then see that a button for developer tools appears at the top of the window in your outlook homepage.

3. Go to the Developer Tools tab and start-up Visual Basic scripting.

4. Here create a new module and script your code to save incoming attachments.

Easy Peasy.

In the example below, the report name is always the subject of the automated email I get. So I used this criterion to decide the save name of my file and save it to a folder.

(I also make sure to save it with the date so that I know that the destination folder always has the most recent file before executing my script.)

VBA script to save attachments on incoming email:

Public Sub SaveRegularReports(MItem As Outlook.MailItem)Dim oAttachment As Outlook.AttachmentDim sSaveFolder As StringsSaveFolder = "/You/want/to/save/here/"If MItem.Subject = "Quarterly_rep" Thensavename = "QRep"ElseIf MItem.Subject = "JustSomeRegRep" Thensavename = "RegRep"ElseIf MItem.Subject = "WeeklyReport" Thensavename = "WeeklyUpdate"End IfFor Each oAttachment In MItem.AttachmentsoAttachment.SaveAsFile sSaveFolder & savename & Format(MItem.ReceivedTime, "mmddyyyy") & ".xlsx"NextEnd Sub

Now, all I have to do is decide whom to apply these rules to. So I go to rules and set up the conditions I want to apply the script to.

In my example, I have the conditions that the email always comes from one specific address with a subject containing the name of the report. (I moved all such regular reports to a separate folder in the inbox for safety)

In the next step, As an action, I execute the above script SaveRegularReports whenever this folder gets a newbie.

Voila! Autosaved everything I need without lifting a finger!!

Step 2. Write a bat file to execute your python script on saved excel sheets

This is also a piece of cake. A bat (or batch) file is a set of commands that get executed by the command prompt when you click it. If you ever wrote bash commands or used the windows command prompt shell scripts to “pip install” and the like, you know what I’m talking about.

Open up a text file and write commands similar to those below to execute your python script, as you would on the command prompt. (I am using Anaconda, So I first activate the anaconda Prompt before executing my python script).

Save this file as filename.bat

@echooff

call C:\Users\myUSer\AppData\Local\anaconda\Scripts\activate.bat

C:\This\is\Where\Your\Script\Lives\Scriptchen.py

pause

When I click on the above bat file (scriptchens_bat.bat), my python script gets executed. It uses the saved excel reports from step 1.

Step 3. Task-Schedule your script execution

Note: I know I receive my reports every week, say on Friday at 10 AM. I set this script to run every Friday at 10:30.

Open up the Task scheduler from the search bar in Windows. Go to Create Basic Task.

After naming and describing it in the appearing window, go to trigger pane and set up your schedule. In this example to 10:30, Fridays.

Now comes the meaty part. Go to the Actions pane. Here is where you set your bat file up for execution. Select “Start Program” from actions, and in the program pane, give the path to your .bat file you wrote in step 2.

click ok and confirm.

Voila! you will now have output of your Python script every week without lifting a finger!

Step 4. Emailing the Results
