Food prices are variable, but I found it cheaper than in Germany. So London might even be more affordable than, say, Munich. The city has my heart. ˚ʚ♡ɞ˚.

title: How Acronyms Kill Office Productivity
content:
How Acronyms Kill Office Productivity

Do the BIM of STS-9/2 at the S-HiL after your PTO

Did you ever sit in a meeting wondering what the hell the guy just said, but stopped yourself from interrupting because you thought it’ll break the flow? Did it involve a godforsaken abbreviation? You already know what I am talking about. This is an overdue problem that needs to be addressed — NOW!

Abbreviations kill all possible logic that can arise out of plain communication. Throw in some office jargon, and you have distasteful conversations that push your human-sensibilities away and hinder your ability to react and respond in a blink. An example:

“I will Circle back to you ASAP once we get the ball rolling on QGR from S-Rep-InSYS”

and this:

“I’ll answer soon as I have the Quarterly Growth Report from the Sales Representative Information System”

Which of the two above formulations does not make you want to rip your eyeballs out?Remember, you talk and think more than you powerpoint or type!

Let’s take a moment to see what good the abbreviations do:

They are a sort of “security” against competitors who spy on you digitally since acronyms are insider-info

They look better on powerpoints.

End Of Reasons.

If code-language is your go-to security feature, and you are not living in the 1800s, maybe you deserve to beJulian Assangedby the genius eves-dropping on you at the red-telephone-box in your office.

As for 2,Why don’t we then only restrict the use of abbreviations for presentations where a human presenter talks in-person to a human listener where there is no scope for possible confusion?

The most damage is incurred by the newbie who enters the workplace fresh out of college, and sits through days of training and first-time meetings, listening to capital letters put together for no reason at all. The poor guy doesn’t interrupt and ask either because of the fear of looking stupid or of rudely interrupting. What do we have as a result? — hours and hours of paid employee time that might as well have been flushed down the toilet. He tries to Google though and is surprised that there are things in the world that don’t come up in the first 3 pages of google’s result (That was the first time he tried going beyond the first page).

The poor guy doesn’t interrupt and ask either because of the fear of looking stupid or of rudely interrupting. What do we have as a result? — hours and hours of paid employee time that might as well have been flushed down the toilet.

This new employee hopes that it gets better with time and goes on to integrate himself into the rusted cog-wheels of the system. Does the problem go away? Hell, no! There is he is, 5 years into the system, a grey-haired colleague walks in and introduces a new abbreviation. Having been in these situations often enough he asks the gray-haired colleague — “What does GERMA stand for?”to which he gets a reply “It is a system that performs this report, I have been in the firm 20 years now, and I have NO idea what that stands for”. “Oh well, ok then! GERMA it is!”

It is a system that performs this report, I have been in the firm 20 years now, and I have NO idea what that stands for

Before you strike this off, hear me out. There are good acronyms and there are bad acronyms. There are those, for example, 24/7, VIP, WTF, which have a universal meaning and help communication massively, and there are office acronyms. FYI, Elon Musk, the god of productivity has issued official orders banning all use of acronyms in his entire organization unless explicitly pre-approved by him. Here is what he has got to say:

There is a creeping tendency to to use made-up acronyms at SpaceX. Excessive use of made-up acronyms is an impediment to communication and keeping communication good as we grow is incredibly important.

The key test for an acronym is to see whether it helps or hurts communication. For example, there should be no HTS and VTS for horizontal or vertical test stand. Those are particularly dumb as they have unnecessary words. VTS-3, for example, is four syllables as compared to a tripod, which is two. So the bloody acronym takes longer to say! It is ok to make up a few acronyms here and there assuming I have approved them, but these should be kept to a minimum.

If you are a boss and you are reading this, please do your organization a favor and save yourself some money too. If you are an employee and you’re reading this, do all you can to stop this poisonous culture from propagating.

Yours Frustrated,

Acronym averse

title: About Me — Sruthi Korlakunta
content:
About Me — Sruthi Korlakunta

I think of myself as Pac-man collecting pointless dots on its way to certain death.

Hi. Nice to meet you, what’s your name?

(Also,n2mywynis my current ear-worm. Feel free to let it play in the BG while you read further.)

Will this bite me in the backside when I’m famous?

I’ve recently established a foolproof benchmark of self-evaluation.

I ask myself this: “Will this bite me in the backside when I’m famous?”.Think about how would it look on the title page ofThe New Yorker.

I ask myself that when I am about to do what the demon on my shoulder just told me.Happens way more often than it should, btw.

Yesterday, he ordered me to give myself a larger scoop of dessert than what I gave my guests. I smudged the scoop on my plate and broke it into two so that the others don’t notice that mine is bigger. I wouldn’t want that in the New Yorker. But I did it anyway.

Damn,I think about these things way more than I should!

Beliefs

I want to be a believer really bad.I don’t care what, butI’d LOVE to believe.

Maybe that pop-tart molecules were the first created post-big-bang. Or that love, hate, want and such have a meaning. I want to believe that there is someone watching out for me, that I am a good person.

I’ll take anything as long as I am convinced.Please convince me so that I can sleep better.

The tragedy is that I’m clinically skeptical.

Skeptical to a degree that when I get an email response or private note, I immediately scan for hints that it is auto-generated.

Unless I see something as obvious as a switch-bulb reaction, I’m incapable of believing and/or deducing cause and effect.

I’d just be happy to see that I have those nerve cells which cause a feeling(or whatever)called belief.

I’m awannabeliever.

Am I pretty?

I am… above this

(punch-line credits: Raphael Bob Waksberg)

is what I tell myself when I come across this question (often and ironically posed also by yours truly).

In the recent past, I was shocked to realize that this answer reveals more serious personality flaws than a regular modest but insipid answer would — like “Everyone is pretty in their own way”.

So now the question is “Am I conceited?” (probably yes).

But I was a refugee from absolute-lack-of-self-esteemia, swam across the anxiety ocean and walked through the depression desert, and illegally crossed the border to conceit-land.

I certainly have no plans of leaving.

Why do I write and What do I write about?

Great question!(**pats self on the back**)

I think of myself as Pac-man collecting pointless dots on his way to certain death.

Death by a monster, by the natural end of pre-programmed playable levels, or simply because my mom decided to pull the plug on little me since I’ve been getting too much screen time.

One of those dots I collect is writing, aka how I spend my time trying to avoid the other monsters. Also, it triggers a reward function in my brain when I write.“I survived another level (day) and I have ALL the dots there are to have for today!”.

This figure down here about sums it up.

What’s my goal in life

I think life is like trying to balance two sides of a scale with exactly the same amount of sand. You are always some grains of sand short on one side. And then you start over.

That’s what I try to do on a daily basis.

I want to be the normal-est, mediocre-est, basic-est person in the world. I want to balance every bit of humbuggery (Not to be confused with regular buggery) and Ned-Flandersary to be “normal”.

Not a backside cavity, not a space-robot-rocket-scientist-investment-rockstar, not the life of the party, and not a bummer. Just the most basic creature in creation- N.O.R.M.A.L.

Turns out it's really hard. So far, I adapted it to my insta-handle. It’s German for TheMostNormal_Person:

ganznormaler_mensch.

Favorite quotes

This about sums up what gets me.

Spoiler alert, It’s mostly existential crisis.

1. Bojack Horseman

“The universe is a cruel, uncaring void. The key to being happy isn’t to search for meaning, it’s to keep yourself busy with unimportant nonsense, and eventually you’ll be dead.”

- Mr. Peanut Butter

2. Community

Oh God no, I never hope. Hope is pouting in advance. Hope is faith’s richer, bitchier sister. Hope is the deformed addict bound incest monster offspring of entitlement and fear.

Dan Harmon

3. Rick and Morty

Rick:I can make a clone of you. A perfect instance of you, an exact copy of you in every way. It’ll love and provide for the kids and do your job and consume broadcast network reality TV on the same allegedly ironic levels as you. You could be gone a day, a week, or the rest of your life with zero consequences. The moment you decide to come back, I flip a switch and the clone’s job is done.

Beth:I don’t know if can do it.

Rick:Then stay, and luxuriate in a life that you finally you know you have chosen. My secret bonus is that no matter what you choose, you are finally going to chill the fuck out.

Also, I lost it when news anchor Tom Jumbo Grumbo reported Bojack stealing a meal from neal Mc Beal the Navy seal. I think it is the best joke on the planet.

So that’s about me, Sruthi.

Do you want to connect? At this point, it is really your own doing. Do ithere.

Fin.

I write here onMedium.

Here is the rest of my socialSruthi Korlakunta. I’m happy to connect!

title: The Urge to Go Home
content:
The Urge to Go Home

What happens to those of us who feel eternally “not home”

I never understood homesickness.

As far as I can remember, I did everything I could tonotgo home. I stayed in an empty school after school hours. I missed the school bus so that I could walk home and arrive later. Every time a long-distance car journey was coming to an end, my heart sank a little as the view of our house drew closer.

My family moved more times than I can count. Every house was a shell on a snail that could fall out any day. I made friends to play with outside, but only I knew that they weren’t permanent. My friends, they went about life like I was going to be in it forever — taking me on their adventures, confiding in me… But I knew. They were just a part of the scenery, and they’d disappear.

I hadn’t thought about home for many years after permanently moving out of the country. It was mostly a relief, except for the occasional feeling that should I fall, it was an abyss under me.

I had no urge to go “home”, in the sense of going back to the familiar world I grew up in. But the urge to go “home” where I belong has always existed. People came and went — friends, acquaintances, roommates, boys I dated, love. With every new connection, one would think the sense of “home” would deepen and grasp me firmer at the root. But I only wriggled and writhed to be free, so that someday, I can go home. Other people looked like home from afar, but as I got closer, I got claustrophobic — like I came to “a” home, but the walls were closing in.

In the 11 years of living by myself, I’ve lived in 12 houses. Somewhere around the 6th, I had decided it would be the last but one. The next and final one would be a home with a family I build, where my children would grow up, and I’d feel at home there. But life had other plans, and 6 homes later, I left the country again.

Now I’m married. I am not used to being married.

I find myself constantly wondering if I’m home enough to be homesick when I go away from here. If this is what it is supposed to feel like, or if I’m missing out on anything that could make this perfect.

And then one day it happened. A flash of recognition, I remembered that time when the sun would fall in a certain way, the temperature exactly right, how I stood in the wind, and how I felt in my body. I longed for those moments I lived. I was finally sad that I was not somewhere. I was finally homesick.

The wriggling and writhing, the little joys I failed to acknowledge then, the joys I don’t acknowledge right now, even those that I do, and the expectation for a future and the confusion in it all — I am homesick for the moments of being that have already been.

It didn’t matter that they weren’t all great, or that I made mistakes that I can’t bury till my grave. It didn’t matter that people from there left too, and I started over again. But it was all me. and that mattered.

Maybe home has nothing to do with other people. Maybe it is just this bag of moments we carry around, with some confidence that we made them ourselves, that we had a part to play in the story we wrote by ourselves.

Maybe to feel at home, agency of your life is all you need.

I may not be finally home, but I am finally homesick — I will take this win.

title: 4 Things to Accept for Mentally Healthy Weight Loss
content:
4 Things to Accept for Mentally Healthy Weight Loss

Love your body even if you are over-weight, they say. Body positivity, they call it. Not acknowledging it is empowerment they write. You have been brain-washed, they said repeatedly.

One side of the world admonishes you for thinking about weight loss. The other side shames you into it. It is no surprise that the process, as a result, is all the more psychologically damning than it is by itself.

The psychological acceptance of it is if not more, as important as every physical change you make to your food and work-out habits.

Here is a set of 5 unchanging and unmoving rules that mentally helped me accept the process. The psychological acceptance of it is if not more, as important as every physical change you make to your food and work-out habits. It is the cornerstone to keep you on your long and tiring track without flinching.

It did not happen overnight, it won’t unhappen overnight

You are not going to be thin by morning, or the morning next week or the week after. Your body gave you a good amount of time to get where it is, and it is going to take its own sweet time to go back. No, it is not being a d*ick, it is doing everything in its strength to keep you alive. Give it a cheer and keep at your workout even when it doesn’t seem to work at all.

Whenever you feel like nothing is changing, remember, It did not happen overnight, it won’t unhappen overnight.

Whenever you feel like nothing is changing, remember, it did not happen overnight, it won’t unhappen overnight. Give itat leastas long as it took to go one way. Accept it and repeat it to yourself.

This is going to cost you time. Probably a lot

There is really only one major investment in losing weight. Time. Yes, you have to cut down your time on your non-active hobbies. Yes, it takes longer to walk than to bike. Yes, it takes up your precious summer evening to go to the gym. Yes it takes time to see results.

Yes it takes time to see results. But that is all! That is the only real investment in this game.

But that is all! That is the only real investment in this game. Accept this fact and invest in it. Accept that there is a reason why you have less time for other things. Understand that if all your hobbies are inactive, You might have to let go of one in the long run. Acceptance is the key.

If you don’t have it, you can’t eat it

You possibly crave sugars or other food-groups on which you depend more than you should. Remember to not walk these aisles in the supermarket. If you are tempted to pick up just one candy bar, pick something sweet in the produce section instead.

This however is only going to be a problem at the beginning. You will find yourself avoiding the sweet section automatically very soon. Remind yourself every time you go shopping, ALL your eating decisions are made in the supermarket.

Your body is not the only thing you manage

You probably have a job, a school to go to, a child, or another responsibility or goal. You are the CEO of your life — a boss, one of whose departments includes body. A body that you have decided currently needs special care.

You are the CEO of your life — a boss, one of whose departments includes body.

There is no logical reason to identify yourself wholly with your physical appearance. It is no doubt an important aspect of you but learn to derive your self-esteem from everything you do. This will improve not only your body but also everything else. It is YOUR project and yours only. But it is also not your only project.

Acceptance and willingness are the key resources to keep your mental state intact through tumultuous endeavors. Especially those whose returns show only after considerable investment. It is as important to develop and maintain the mental resources needed on this journey as it is physical.

title: I Went Homeless for a Week
content:
I Went Homeless for a Week

Here is how it was.

Before you go on about how this is not“True homelessness”,I am aware. I was well equipped. I just didn't go home.

My goal is not to claim that I know what it is like to be homeless. Or that I have suffered like a homeless person. Far from it, I have people who love me, whether I like it or not. I have a good job, and my boss is an angel. My relationships are mostly with people who are all better than myself —  Kind, empathetic, loyal, and giving. I don’t have unsolvable problems in my life. I don't have a sob story that compares to what most people experience by the default shittiness that is life.

The reason I“went homeless”is because I feel homeless nevertheless. It has to do with the relationship I have with myself. I don’t have a list of things you should learn from this experience, here it is anyway.

What I had

I had my phone and the means to charge it.I had moneyMy car was my home. (Small-sized sport-back, foldable seats at the back for sleeping)I went to work as usual, did no over-hours to have a roof over my head.I showered at the local gym where I was already a member.I took enough pairs of clothes in the boot of my car.I carried pepper spray.I shopped at a local supermarket for food and water.PS:It is legal in Germany to sleep in a car.

The weather

It is February in Germany. So the weather is cold.

What it costed

What I missed

Where I slept

VillageCityForestCampsiteParking lot

What I did not miss

What it felt like

I picked my adventure because I didn't like the ones that came naturally to me. I know me more.

title: Don’t Wait To Retire, Live Now
content:
Don’t Save Only To Retire, Live Now

Your FoMo is real, life IS really passing by, the future you has no interest in the same things.

More than once, I read an email about a colleague who had just passed away. These emails always hit the hardest when you find out they had retired just the year before. All those 401 (k) s, retirement home investments, insurance, and savings plans for an entire lifetime being for absolutely nothing is one of my worst nightmares.

I’m Indian. To boomer Indian parents, the idea of life wasn’t the past, the present, or the future. It was I.My brother and I.Whatever it took them to get us to the other side — independent, educated, potentiallyrich,that was their idea of life. As a result, I have more memories of angry, unfulfilled, and dissatisfied family dynamics and 4K-FullHD mental pictures of my marks cards than of any other moment in my life — Until I left home, that is.

That was when the world opened up. I collected experiences that defined me and my life at a near-zero price. Everything I did after I left home for an education in Germany was so formative and profound that I barely have memories of anything that came before.

Now I’m 30 and acutely aware of how my body is changing. The diminishing interest in the frivolous joys of life, how people, even just a year or two older, prefer the couch to the road, how in many adults my age, sex life is a thing of the past, how as opportunities increase, interest in all-things-life just drops for no reason.

Now, with two lifestyles to compare, I know where the finances vs. experiences debate stands with me. Most traditional “financial advice” taught me how to come out with the largest integer at the end. I politely refuse, but I’m making sure that the “future me” will thank me nevertheless.

Experience Gap

I cut the latte, skipped the flight, and ground through the burnout, but by the time I reached college, I was nothing compared to my worldly peers because of the experience gap I had in understanding the real world.

I see the same gap when I meet with the elderly back home. 65+, and just beginning to travel and see the world, if at all, because life was spent saving for kids who have long left home. Travelling at 65 is great, but by that age, their ways are set, and so are their knees. Trails to be enjoyed from the safety of a funicular, sleeping safely at 8 pm on a bright night in Singapore, and hoping that the car goes right up to the nearest parking spot because we’re tired by noon and still have a whole day planned out.

To beat the experience gap, we need to start treating ourTime-Offwith the same aggressive accounting we use for our 401(k).

The Bucket List

Instead of waiting for a terminal diagnosis or wondering if you can afford a second child, live your life because no one can take away the memories you already made:

Identify "Perishable" Experiences:List all the things you want to do that require physical peak health (e.g., surfing, backpacking, intensive travel). Schedule one for this year, even if it means lowering your savings rate. More importantly, schedule them every week, month, or quarter too, because most of these nature-free-for-alls are actually free. Investing your time is all you really have to do for a good local adventure. Even owning a bicycle will give you a good bang for your buck.

"Die With Zero" (Regrets):When I look at my savings and investments, the smallest of them gives me the most happiness, and the largest of them is but a blurry promise that I’m indifferent to. I own a home where I’ll never live. It made me happy the day I bought it. And now what? I made sure that for every future investment, I will also give myself the liberty to make apresent investment.My next saving is for a holiday, not towards another home.

Buy Back Your Saturdays:If you have the means, stop spending your limited "now" on tasks you hate. Use that extra cash to outsource chores. The best part of living in 2026 is that you can outsource your chores to machines. IF there exists a gadget or an app that lets you buy a service — Dishwashers, robot cleaners, washer-dryers, buy them.

Is travel really that expensive?

“Travel is Expensive” is kind of a lie. You don’t really need a five-star finish line. The whole point of travel is to realize your young years — So why are you worried about all the luxuries that anyway become a need when you are 60?

A meaningful trip does NOT require a massive withdrawal from the “future fund”. An expensive trip is sometimes just a lack of imagination. Whether it’s choosing a season where costs drop, living cheap, or eating realistically instead of at hotel buffets, the world is much cheaper than instagram leads you to believe. The biggest expense is the time you lose waiting. Don’t wait forever.

The key to balancing living and surviving (aka now and later) lies in control. Knowing exactly how much you are NOT spending towards your future — in terms of time, money, and life experience.

Financial security is important.But It is’nt a zero-sum game. Work just as hard to avoid experience-bankruptcy.While money is a renewable resource, your youth, your health, and your “now” are unfortunately not.

title: Stop Pecking At Your Childhood for Pain
content:
Stop Pecking At Your Childhood for Pain

Don’t look for excuses, look for solutions

Chapter 1 — The making of you

Off the bat — Childhood emotional abuse is real. It is hard to get over. Your childhood conditioning plays a huge role in the person you become.

That off my chest, I’d like to continue speaking.

This story is not from a lack of empathy. I spent enough of my life wallowing in my sadness and pain. There was a point in time when not a day went by without panic attacks.

“If only it hadn’t been this way!”,“If only I didn't get bullied”,“ If only my parents did this or that”.

When I say “enough of my life”, I don’t mean a year. I mean decades. That moment when I found out that there is this guy called Freud, I opened a pandora’s box.

I read and read — blog posts, internet advice, books on psychology, Facebook, Reddit. I learned the names of disorders their names, origins, the scientists who proved them, and the methods they used.

I wanted to be thorough in my excuses. I needed proof of my inadequacy. And I needed the proof to tell me that it is not my fault.

Chapter 2 — Empathy

That was my state of mind when I met another person just like me at college. I’ll call her Miriam. I thought I empathized with Miriam. I heard her complain about her parents for hours on.

The problem was, she was at least a decade ahead of me. She had already carried her own trauma for much longer than I have. She had taken 10 years to do a degree which took others 3. She lived off her parents even then.

“If only I didn’t get bullied” was her answer to unfinished laundry, rotting food in the cupboards, 8 years of shutting herself in a room and video gaming, drenching herself in alcohol without having worked for a penny to reach mid-thirties.

Chapter 3 — Epiphany

It happened during a casual stroll with Emily, another friend of mine suffering from obesity.

Emily had a sharp scientific mind and a very human softness about her that made her approachable and empathetic. In a casual talk we drifted off to wanting to be “thin” and she talked about her weight-issues and health.

“My parents forced me to exercise. Having been forced, I hate it more. That is the cause of my weight issues. I hate my parents for that”.

It basically translated to

“My parents cared for me and made me exercise, so I am obese”.

It was a momentous discovery for my mind which was already taking to excuses like a fish to water.

People find their childhood imperfect, regardless of what their parents do!

Chapter 4 — Entitlement

This Reminds me of that time in India, when the country was drowning under a flood, a millionaire’s sonpushed his brand-new BMW into a river, because he didn’t get the Jaguar that he asked for. Of course!

Entitlement flows in our nerves with the blood.

There is no checklist for parents where work their way for the “perfect parenting” trophy. The “injustice” that is being jammed down our throat every single day was also what they swallowed in their day — only in their floppy-disk-mainframe-shitty version.

What we know to be “obvious parenting knowledge” today was not a thing. Their own parents grew up in a world where pregnant women were encouraged by lung doctors to exercise their lungs by smoking.

Beating up a kid wasgoodpunishment. The world was tough. And a world like that makes an animal out of man.

Chapter 5 — 24 hours

This is no excuse for abuse— emotional or otherwise. But all said and done, the question that you answer for yourself every single moment of life is

“Now what?”

There is really one thing you know for sure. There are 24 hours in a day. For you, me, and everyone else on the planet.

You can use your waking hours either to either know more, research more, and find every excuse under the sun for why you are not the cause of your perceived inadequacy.

OR

Now that you looked enough for reasons and causes, you can put that project to rest and go on and use your hours and thoughts to change that. On a given day, you can either blame or grow. You cannot do both.

Remember,there are only 24 hours in a day.

Chapter 6 — Acceptance

There are no changing the facts. The abuse we suffer is utterly inexcusable. People are imperfect. You were made to suffer purely out of spite and hatred which you didn't do anything to deserve.

Now what?

You pick up off the floor and you move on.

You need family and friends. If you don’t have one or the other, you move on and find new. There is a practically infinite human resource on this planet. There are great people around, you just have to reach out.

You need courage and confidence. If you don't have them, you work with what you have — diligence and determination.

You will look back to your past and find excuses — but this time for your parents and friends, who didn't know better. Whom you either accept or forget, because either you are over it or they don't matter anymore.

Will you then, never fail? Sure you will. You will fall and you will tumble. Most importantly, you will get back up. Nothing deadfalls or gets back up. It stays where it is. But you, you will fall again, and then get back up again. Because that is what people do. They get back up.

title: How I did not meet my Husband
content:
How I Did Not Meet My Husband

About being 31, Unmarried, a Woman, and Indian.

“Ah, she’s not worth the gossip, she is over 30 already, and not in shape.”

I remember that conversation with my married Indian friends about one of our friends from college. I was 29 then, and that was 2 years ago.

Now I’m 31. My married friends are having babies. The last of the unmarried ones married the nearest perfect match like it was musical chairs. (No judgment here, life must go on the way you like). The still unmarried ones have disappeared from the radar as if society has banished them.

I am one of the few remaining ones still in the pool and afraid to take the plunge.

What it feels like?

While Single at 29 the world is still curious,you are still full of expectations. Men lay a bed of roses for you to walk until it gets to a “Yes”. You are the hot topic of the market, because“you’re next”.

Single at 30 is scary.Friends and family start eyeing you with suspicion. The married ones no longer hang out with you the same way. Men in the pool are worried that women are hiding a child behind them, and the women are worried that they have to settle for less“just because”.

Unlike at 21, at 30, the subject of marriage comes forth, and it gets uncomfortable to“date and see where it goes”.

At 31 and single, the world is normal again.You realize that nothing has changed really. You aren’t going to be highlighted by a neon hallow that says you’re 31 and single.

Things are exactly the same as they used to be, the future is still a long way to go (at 30, you somehow believe that life ends at 31), and that being married, though not compulsory is quite convenient.

Why would you still be single at 30?

I remember at 21 when my dad brought me a match to marry, without consulting me.

I neither registered the meaning nor need of being married. After an uphill battle, my dad never interfered in the matters of my mind again.

Since then I have met amazing people who have shown me that existence could be bliss. That the world out there is bigger than exchanging assets, building wealth, and achievements. Life incurred meaning in a way I thought only existed in books.

I found out what love feels like, what it means to a creature like me when someone believes and trusts in me. That was an a trophy that I could win by playing the game, not on Shaadi.com.

Since I started choosing by myself, I have only had good people in my life. Today, I am proud to say that I know 0 people who aren’t worth it.Then what happened?

Unfortunately, my limited life and experience have not taught me what it means to plan a future, to aspire to a life together, to build a world in which I can also give, and not just take.

To give needs much more strength, maturity, balance of mind, and courage than to take. In a relationship, you can never give enough.

During my search for a partner, I met people with astonishing strength to give, but I have not had the same strength in me. Hence, this story.

Did I do something about it?

I tried.I was on apps(click for story)and met some great people, but always ended up suspicious of the full story.

I put myself on Indian matrimonial websites (click for story),only to discover that I am not cut out for a search like that.

I met one really lovely boy through mutual friends, to discover that my strength to give hasn’t matured.

To be fair, The world is easier for women than men. But the heart obliges to partners more easily in men than women.

What comes next?

I don't know.

The goal of my life right now is to be comfortable not knowing.

I have seen too many relationships that “exist” and don't function, to want that for myself. So I refuse to plan roads to marriage.

I no longer want to build a relationship, I want to build a two-person team that manages a project of“Squeezing the most out of life.”

I want to be able to see the good in people, and not ask“What is in it for me?”I do not want to accept anything less than that for myself either.

If it leads to a relationship, it's a great story. If it doesn’t, well, dear reader, remember to drop me a message on my 90th birthday and tell me that I am not alone, because you read this, and you know me.

If you liked this, you probably will also like these:

I am Sruthi. Join Medium with my referral link, It costs $5 a month and changed my life for good.

title: Short Story: Life Has Meaning
content:
Short Story: Life Has Meaning

I remember when Michaela flung her glass of red wine at the beige stucco wall and broke down in a pile of tears. I’ve known her and George since we were children. They were not a perfect couple.

Michaela reminded George every day how he can do a little bit better. He tried his best but somehow always managed to do the wrong thing. They squabbled, argued, went on holidays together, and quarreled on the beach. But in the Fall of 2012, he popped the question and she said yes.

I was at their wedding. It felt like we always knew we’d see this day. Michaela and George belonged together. And Just like that, frenemies of 18 years turned to newlyweds.

Not long after that, one Sunday morning, George took Bella on a walk and never returned. They found him on the way to the gas station. They said that the truck driver was drunk and on Youtube.

They said that George could hear and see everything, maybe even understand it, we can never tell. Bella would never come back.

If George understood anything, he never showed a sign. Michaela sat by his side every day for a couple of weeks, until one day, George passed.

Everyone else thought that it was the kinder thing to happen but no one said that out loud. It was like something permanently broke inside of Michaela.

For months, Michaela locked herself up in her and George’s new home. It was hard to tell if she was listening when friends and family talked to her. She hardly left her living room and did not call anyone.

But one day, I saw Michaela on her way home from the supermarket. She had been drinking. Nevertheless, I was glad to see her up and about again. I tried to persuade her to visit me that weekend. Since she never showed up, I drove to her’s.

That day on, my visits became regular. Sometimes, I brought along some common friends. Gradually, we could convince her to leave the home and see people. But hardly any meeting ended without Michaela leaving abruptly and in tears.

I did not believe that she could ever be the same person again. If I had lost a part of me one fine morning, I don’t know if I would stand up and fight. Most of all, I don’t know why I would try. So I understood.

The winter after George’s passing, I accompanied Michaela to the animal shelter. The loneliness of the large suburban home was too much for her to bear. I think getting a dog was a good decision.

The shelter was stuffed to gills with abandoned pets. The people at the shelter had to take every newcomer, no matter how little space they had. It was a rather disheartening sight. We were glad we would bring home a friend.

As we stood looking around, a large German shepherd laid himself at Michaela’s feet, adopting every dogly expression to tell her that he loved her. After a very long time, I saw love return to Michaela’s face. She touched him as if they always knew each other. Milo beamed with large eyes.

As we signed the paperwork for Milo we found out that he had one day before the shelter put him permanently to sleep. They needed to make space and fast, and unadopted pets get only so much shelf life.

Michaela pressed Milo to her chest and took him to his new home. I don’t know who saved whom.

Since Milo, Michaela recovered miraculously. His joy became hers. Where previously nothing could reach her, Michaela now laughed out loud during a simple game of fetch.

Milo and Michaela were a family. Sometimes, they also let strangers in. I could tell that that was going to be the beginning of something good. As Michaela cared for milo, she cared for other things that didn’t previously bother her. She went on walks and to the vet. She went back to caring about the trivial world outside.

7 years since all that, Milo is a part of that family of two beautiful little boys and a loving husband who, unfortunately, doesn’t take as well to the dog. But Milo is going to be there for her, and Michaela for him.

In the deepest of my despair, I look at that home as a stranger and an irregular visitor, and I often think, There is always something out there to live for. Death does not kill everything.

title: My Heavily Filtered, Duck-Faced Instagram Alter-Ego — Curly Sko
content:
My Heavily Filtered, Duck-Faced Instagram Alter-Ego — Curly Sko

vs. my nearly-real self.

PS: MyYouTube Channelof the same name has nothing to do with this article. She talks about hair care and she is really cool :) This is about my Insta-persona.

Hi. Nice to meet you. I amganz_normalermensch(That’s German for a very normal person).

The girl in those pictures was curly_sko. She was my flamboyant Instagram alter-ego. Shewas,because I had to end her after 2 months of activity. She was cool though.

When curly_sko was born, I was in a tough place. I had little around me which felt meaningful. I held on to myself as much as possible.

This is a little window into my alter-ego.

Instagram image and truth

Curly_sko got me worried about whether I really am that girl. Whether I really looked and thought like her. Whether the things that are important to her were important to me.

In most cases, they were not. I didn't look a lot like her, though my face was the greenscreen for her Instagram filters. I suppose it is the same with most pictures on social media.

It is not just the filters. Still-images come from specific angles, both literally and metaphorically. Curly_sko left me feeling like I am not giving the world a real picture of me. When I tried to correct that, I felt like there is really no need to do so. Not everyone on Instagram is your friend.

So here is what I was left with — Tell the truth and be vulnerable to strangers. Or be a half-truth and mislead people. Out of two bad options, the latter is the better one. These are the choices everyone has.

Everything on insta is the tip of the iceberg. Sometimes even the opposite of the truth.

Self Obsession and Self Love.

There is a zone where self-love gradient-dissolves into self-obsession. Self-love is quiet. Self-love accommodates others and makes room for love. Self-love doesn’t rub it in others’ faces and doesn’t judge.

On the contrary, self-obsession has no room for anyone else. It stems from fear, from clutching too tightly to self and identity. For the fear that you are not who you think you are.

Curly_sko was self-obsessed. But I don’t really blame or diss her for it. You have one life, live out all your personas.

If you worry too much about yourself and not the people and events around you, think about changing them. The world is full of kindness in places you would least expect. Seek and reach out, but be careful who you reach out to.

Why that face?

When things are going great it is easy to love yourself and others. When they aren’t, you fall into a major self-defense and self-appraisal mode (which might occasionally spill over to self-obsession).

That is probably why curly_sko was born. I never felt a greater need to look at myself and say “It’s okay, come on!”

The greatest thing I learned from this is that for a healthy mind, it is important to surround yourself with good people. In times of good mental health (We all have our seasons) be someone else’s good person. Enough people kick others, and we all have enough of that.

It is a trending “intellectual-person” quirk to look down on influencers, on people who try. There is really no need.

Not all Influencer-business is a result of temporary mental illness. But usually, there is a product, some content. If the only product is the person, be kind, don’t kick them.

If you have a personality inside you that you want to live out, I recommend getting an anonymous account and doing it. Your friends will still find you. So you should still watch what you say. You don’t know until you try ;)

I am Sruthi Korlakunta, read me onMedium, or write me onLinkedInIf you have an idea or a story to share, hit me up! I have 880+ followers and over 220k reads and counting.

If I convinced you to become a member on medium,join herewith my referral link.

title: The Unsung Heroes of Glass Skin Routines
content:
The Unsung Heroes of Glass Skin Routines

The biggest factors are those they never talk about

Before I give you all the “secrets,” let us be very clear. Glass skin is not a state of being. It is a brief few days/day/, or even hours. It is not something you rock day and night. There’s a reason they say “good hair day”, and similarly, it will at best be a “glass skin day”.

I’ve chased the trend since I first heard the word “Glass skin”. So many serums and masks, so much money out the window, and so much research!

Growing up in an Indian household with Curcuma and yoghurt masks, big, blue Nivea “cold-cream” (a term that has since vanished) boxes, naturally, in my early 30s, I’m determined never to let my skin age.

At the beginning, I bought all the chemical exfoliants, “active” serums, anything that had complicated names on it (think niacinamide, hyaluronic acid, retinol, and all the things I hadn’t ever heard of until 2019). But achieving the brief glass-skin state for a day or two taught me a few valuable lessons.

The biggest heroes are those they never talk about

Beyond a simple topical skin care routine, which makes up perhaps a teeny little chunk of the skin health, I think these are the largest factors for a good skin day.

Gym. Run. Sweat. A few vitamins.

I took up consistent strength training last year. Incidentally, that was also when all the skin care products were letting me down, and letting me breakout and scar. (Pic under, a mark that was left by over-exfoliating to the right of my mouth)(Also read: The Skin Care Scam)

This battle scar had stayed with me for the better part of the year. It faded completely in the first few months I began to work out.

I can hear you groan through the screen. Of course,no one can be a 100% perfect at keeping a schedule while also working a full-time job, raising kids, taking care of a home, and whatnot.

The trick to making these a part of your life is to keep failing at doing them.

We aren’t meant to be perfect; we are meant to be constantly chasing a better life. And that’s not a bad thing. There is beauty and purpose in it. That applies to foodandworkout habits.

You will have off weeks, off months, and off years, even. I personally had several. And your skin may very well suck then. But don’t stop trying. Just like good skin days, great habits are also not a constant, and that is ok. Doesn’t make them any less responsible for your skin health.

Want great skin? Go work out that week.

Superfoods

The thing about superfoods that I noticed is that they might be an overkill for your pocket, but usually, they don’t damage my health. Some trend or the other didn’t deliver what it promised, but I’m always happy to try a new food that didn’t come from a plastic bag.

I’ve bought chia seeds, goji berries, and went on the avocado trend for a while — I’ve been through all that shebang. Some really worked. Some made me bloated. Some gave me a few good skin days.

Chia seeds, for example, were my love-hate situationship.(Also read: The Chia seed Fallacy)

I did, in the process, discover one skin superfood that seems to work, at least for this month.

Of course,eating a healthy diet is the biggest piece of the puzzle. APART from that, it helped to take a spoonful of Amla (gooseberry) juice every morning.

I notice that it has made my skin clearer, although I cannot be certain that it is just the juice, and not juice+gym+skin routine (I was diligent with all of them).

I mixed Amla juice with Aloe vera juice and took it with warm water every morning for about 10 days before having some glass skin days. I was also regular with my omega-3 supplements. I notice that my skin has a natural shine whenever I am regular with omega-3s.

Mind you, when I take all the supplements regularly but eat like a 5-year-old and lie in bed, my skin does NOT behave.

Summary, These foods helped: Amla juice, omega-3 supplements.

Skin care routine

One fad after another, it is hard to keep up. I went through so many products, my skin was overwhelmed, broke out, gave up, recovered, and then all that again.

I finally settled on the simplest routine because I was mentally tired of doing chemistry equations in my head. Also because I found too many vitamin C serums lying in my closet untouched.

Here’s what I did. Brands don't matter, but I will write mine anyway.

Morning:

Vitamin C serum (I used CeraVe)

Moisturizer (I used OLEHENRIKSEN’s Strength Trainer Peptide Boost Moisturizer)

Sunscreen (I used a drugstore sunscreen from dm (Germany) called sundance.

Evening:

Vitamin C serum

Moisturizer

Sometimes I do a skincare evening as an “us-time” for my husband and me. I used BHA exfoliant and then the same evening routine as above (no more than once a week) or a DIY physical exfoliant — Chickpea flour+ Yoghurt + Turmeric, just like in my childhood. These definitely add the extra boost my clear skin needs.

The BHA exfoliant routine makes the skin shiny, whereas the DIY makes it matte. But they both show on the skin the next day.

Mind you, every time I did any of these exfoliants more than once a week, I broke out in acne, and I’m not even acne-prone!

Also, all the active ingredients and super Korean magic skincare stuff didn’t really work enough to justify their price.

Sleep

As a chronic insomniac, I was really impressed by what happens when I don’t give in to my phone at night. The results are truly life-changing.

I have had the best sleep of my last decade since I randomly didn’t reach for my phone one day, and I haven’t gone back in a month. I’m certain this has added to my great skin days immensely.

These were my skin-care results for the month.

To refresh, tl;dr:Gym, Amla, Omega-3, Vitamin C serum, Moisturizer and sunscreen!

Hope it helps!

title: The Best MacBook and iPad for the Average User (For the Price-Sensitive)
content:
The Best MacBook and iPad for the Average User (For the Price-Sensitive)

iPad Pro? or Air? or 5ᵗʰ Gen? or 4ᵗʰ Gen? Should you just get a MacBook instead?

This article will help you identify your needs and budget, and match them to an iDevice. At least if you have similar needs to mine. This is not a full technical review, there are hundreds of Youtube videos and articles on the web for that. :)

I have been hunting for the best price-performance deals on a MacBook and/or an iPad for nearly half a year now. Full disclosure, other than an iPhone, this would be my first Apple Computer. About my buying attitude — I am definitely a careful spender. Having said that, I don’t mind spending as long as I know I am getting the best for the price.

I wanted my Apple (iPad? MacBook?) to be able to do the following

Access my email, social media, and such drab work. I’d probably use these features a lot.

Write on Medium. Also a lot.

Design on Canva.

Do my Coursera courses and some personal Python Projects.

Possibly some drawing tools, but nothing very fancy. Mostly experimental.

On occasion, surfing charts and graphs and zoom into positions on a bigger screen.

Definitely something with 256 GB, at least.

Basically, I wanted BOTH tablet and laptop functionality.

Once I started the hunt, the decision wasn’t easy. The best is always way too expensive to be reasonable. To compromise on the second best is a difficult choice. To make me feel that way is exactly how they priced and designed Apple products.

To make matters worse, Samsung released its S9 series the same week, and their devices were quite awesome. Their tablets even came with a pencil, while Apple needed me to buy every accessory.

I started with the iPad Pro

I went to the store hoping to seal the deal with this. Since I wanted to draw, work, and carry around the laptop on the go, I thought the iPad Pro would be the best bet. It is an iPad with an M2 chip — basically a laptop in the form of a tablet.

On closer inspection, the 12’9 inch iPad Pro is too heavy ( nearly 3kg with keyoard) to be a tablet and too narrow to be a computer. I felt like wouldn’t enjoy either experience. The fact that I would have to purchase a keyboard and a pencil, driving the price up to over 2000 Eur (!!!) is simply unacceptable.

Reviews also tell that the iPad is not capable of exploiting the capacity of the M2 chip with iOS in its current form. It cannot replace a laptop.

I dropped the idea of an iPad Pro altogether.

Key Takeaway for iPad Pro:

The iPad Pro is too heavy to be a tab and too small to be a computer.

With a keyboard and a pencil, the price is nearly 2000 Eur (!!!)

Despite the powerful chip, it cannot replace a laptop. The chip uniformity among devices is more for Apple’s management convenience than for the user.

iPad Air 5ᵗʰ Gen, 4ᵗʰ Gen, or iPad 10ᵗʰ Gen, and 9ᵗʰ Gen

The iPad Air is the next device I considered. I started at Gen. 5 and worked my way downwards

Gen. 5 iPad Air

The Gen 5 iPad Air is awesome. But again, the M1 chip isn’t built for a tablet. In this too, the processing capacity of M1 remains unexplored.

Going by the price, I would pay just as much as I’d pay for a MacBook M1 if I get an iPad Air Gen 5.

Personal experience tells me that, of all the devices we own, Tablets are the least used. We love our phones and can sit forever with computers, but tabs are really not going to catch up with that kind of screen time (unless maybe you are a designer or something).

To spend over 1000 euros on a tablet with a capacity of a laptop is not going to make any sense.

By now, I decided that if I wanted an iDevice with both tab and laptop functionality, I’d have to buy both. There is no one-size-fits-all. It doesn’t make sense — price-wise.

Going down further in the iPad world, it became clear to me that every time you compare two consecutive generations,it is going to make more sense to buy one generation older than the latest. Apple offers little upgrades for a massive price.

Not just by the price, but the performance too. In fact, sometimes, older generations tend to even be the better choice. If only they named their devices without numbers or chronology, we’d be making better choices.

For example, The iPad 10th Gen does not support the newer Apple Pencils, you can only use the older Apple Pencils, with a lightning cable. You have to buy an adapter to charge it in the USB Type C ports. Also, the non-laminated screen is uncomfortable to touch. The space between the screen and the device is very palpable.The 9th Gen is hands down, the better device.

If the 9th Gen iPad feels too old, the iPad Air 4th Gen is the best device in this league. With an A14 chip and a 10.9 Liquid Retina display, it's perfect and in under 500 Eur.

Key takeaway:

iPad 10 Gen with no new pencil support and a non-laminated screen is a no-go. The 9th Generation would make even more sense, also financially.

iPad Air 5th Gen is too expensive for my budget. I am ready to spend about Eur. 1200 for a decent Laptop and maybe Eur. 700 for a good tablet.

A refurbed Apple iPad 4th Gen with the A14 Bionic Chip at 470 Eur. is the best choice, in my opinion. (Official Apple refurbishing with a one-year guarantee)

At this point, I have a better understanding and prioritization of my needs for the devices they had on offer.

I decided to get both a MacBook and an iPad. I would get a powerful computer and a relatively weak tablet, to keep the costs low.While I will keep on hunting for a good deal on an iPad, I moved on to the MacBooks. MacBook comes now and an iPad comes later as an addition.

MacBook Pro and MacBook Air

The 2022 MacBook Pro M2 is the flagship model in the MacBooks. For a 13" version, the price starts at 1500 Eur.

Though this is a high price tag, since the device comes with an M2 chip, it might be worth the investment if you need it for intense work. There are also larger screen sizes available, for a starting price of 2400 Eur.

The MacBook Pro is a high-performance laptop that is designed for professionals who need a powerful machine for tasks such as video editing, graphic design, and programming. Since I did not need this level of performance for my jobs, I moved on to the MacBook Airs.

The MacBook Air is lighter and more portable, making it a good choice for less intense tasks and traveling. It comes with M1 & M2 chip configurations.

M1 Chip configuration started at Eur. 950 while the M2 started at Eur. 1300. The M2 chip has an 18% better CPU performance than the M1 chip for the same power consumption and a 35% better GPU performance.

Despite these impressive numbers, the general review from Apple users has been that the M2 chip upgrade is barely noticeable.It doesn't justify the price. The M2 also comes with a liquid retina display, while the M1 comes with a Retina display. Another minor design difference I noticed is the bezel-free design of the M2.

The unbelievably thin design of the MacBook M1 impressed me. The only feature I really missed by not getting a MacBook Air M2 is the Bezel-free design on the screen, which gave me more screen space. I could live with the rest of it without even noticing the difference.

Key Takeaway

This is it. for the price, The M1 Macbook Air is an unbeatable device!

For 950 Eur. you are really getting the best of Apple without compromising on any functionality. (Ok, maybe the touchscreen feature, but you have to draw a line somewhere).

I finally got the MacBook Air M1 with 0 regrets. This is really the best device this money can buy.

title: After Thoughts From the Friends Reunion
content:
After Thoughts From the Friends Reunion

Not every life is a set on friends. It's okay. (Also, no spoilers).

It was hard not to well up from watchingFriends Reunion,just like in the last episode.

F.R.I.E.N.D.S were perfect. The perfect sync in group chats, latching on to each other’s ideas and reacting like they shared the same brain; heck, even breakups are very fixable.

I watched the reunion with wide pupils, a tilted head resting on my palm, and on my lips a smile. I believed every bit of what everybody said. Just like a part of me believed that the story on the show was real.

If my life was a show just like friends, It would be inching towards an end as I now inch towards my thirties.

I look at few moments in my life like I look at the moments in friends.

Real life seemed imperfect. I nearly never get a second chance at a perfect shot. I go home and think of comebacks. I picture how it would have played out. I stay away from people who I think are a handful. Then in a few days, I try to fix the broken patch.

Often, I start over. I open my mind, I close it again, I decide to stay away, and I bounce back into life.

In all the scenes, people are different. The nice ones move out, marry, fly away. Some don’t. But hardly ever does an acquaintance slide smoothly into its place.

For a moment I was dejected.Why does it have to be like this?

Where are all those people I invested years in? Why can’t I have what the F.R.I.E.N.D.S are having?

I then saw what I was doing wrong. I was counting wrong. I have had millions of these moments. Tonnes of them. Buckets of them.

What about the time when I and two of my friends walked the streets around the college for hours? We talked nonsense and laughed aloud. That was my coffee shop, although it has been 12 years, right?

And that time that my friends showed up to move my heavy furniture, though they knew me just for a few months. What if I don’t hang with them every evening anymore? They still pivoted my couch.

And when I met the weird traveler on the car-sharing app? Maybe he is my Mr. Heckles from downstairs. I don’t care if we didn’t write to each other and I didn’t really save his number.

I remember the time when a hobo walked up to me, struck up a one-way conversation for about 2 full hours. That would make for a brilliant scene on friends, wouldn’t it?

The sense of fulfilment that comes withbeing therefor someone is something only you can manufacture. There may not always be the same takers, but surely, there are takers.

We all have our moments. It took me quite a while to realize that I do. Unlike on friends, our days are 24 hours long. I guess it is our job to crush all the moments into bite-sized memories.

Hell, even F.R.I.E.N.D.S could only hold it together 30 minutes at a time. Give it a rest.

title: The Difference Between Rich and Middle-Class Neighborhoods in Germany
content:
The Difference Between Rich and Middle-Class Neighborhoods in Germany

From my experiences living in 12 different homes over 8 years.

After 2 years of living in the rich and quiet part of the Audi-city Ingolstadt, I am moving yet again. In my 8 years in Germany, I lived in about 12 homes in Bremen, Leonberg (Stuttgart), Karlsruhe, Braunschweig, Wolfsburg, Ingolstadt, and Munich. Here is a review of life and living in Germany.

I lived in these arrangements:

My own private studio apartment.

A private shared apartment twice with 3 and 4 flatmates respectively.

A student dorm with 6 others.

In the room of a daughter who moved out, a 65yo elderly lady for a flatmate.

In my brother’s apartment when I couldn’t afford rent.

Home of a friend’s spare bed.

In a hotel.

Home of acquaintances in the heart of Munich which normally would be impossible and cost a bomb.

In the super-rich part of Ingolstadt, on the first floor of an elderly couple in their“Up-movie-type”home, but with a massive garden and a swimming pool.

Now to my next destination, a single apartment. I need more life around me.

Apart from this, I lived for a couple of weeks/months each in Estonia, California, and Berlin for study and, or work. I am not including those experiences to keep it German and simple.

Verdict 1: Germany is not violent at all.

Compare this to Los Angeles where I visited for a weekend and heard Gunshots twice.

Of all these places I lived, some were rich, some were middle class, and one was even poor.

It is not as common in Germany to have a “poor neighborhood” as in many parts of the world. Big cities like Berlin are an exception.

In big cities, homelessness is a common sight and occasional (gun-less) brawls are easy to find. Smaller cities are mostly peaceful.

But Each city had its own neighborhood with a well-known “reputation” —  All-night kiosks, noise, and occasional brawls. These are well known to the locals though not evident to a newbie from the looks of it.

I once lived in one of these “unsafe places" in Bremen.Even then, I walked home for 7 Kilometers in the dead of night at 2 AM feeling quite safe.Compare this to Los Angeles where I visited for a weekend and heard Gunshots twice. Germany is not violent at all.

Verdict 2: Foreigners are still Alien in German Private lives.

Despite Germany’s open arms in its immigrant policies, equal treatment at work, and fair chances for all sexes, the country is still getting used to “the new”.

Walk into any house party, club, office, or shared apartment, and this difference is very evident. Language is only partly responsible for this divide.

Germans have a set of predefined topics that they bond over. These are usually how they spent or plan to spend a holiday, traffic jams on the Autobahn and the time it takes to go places, sports, very carefully planned and executed free-time activities, or something “material” for example, a newly acquired electric grill or aThermomix.

Casual small talk, fooling around, a spontaneous joke, or playful stepping on personal boundaries is not common in German culture. In most other gregarious cultures, these are things that people bond over.

There are places I remember,all my life, though some have changed.Some forever, not for better-some have gone and some remain.All these places have their moments,with lovers and friends, I still can recall.Some are dead and some are living,In my life, I’ve loved them all.

The Beatles.

title: Do Something Now: Ingolstadt for Ukraine.
content:
Do Something Now: Ingolstadt for Ukraine.

If you live in Ingolstadt or Munich, here is how you can help.

A free land is being taken.Ukrainians are not real people, Ukraine is not a real country, Putin declared. For nearly 80 years, Europe has lived in peace. This could change soon. Unless YOU do something.

On Saturday 26th February,Ingolstadt gathered at the Paradeplatzto show Solidarity to Ukraine. Around 800 people took to the street to protest the Russian aggression. Several Ukrainian Nationals and also locals voiced their support and called for a clear stance against the actions of Russia. Germany has since moved — There will be military support to Ukraine. The United States declared severe economic sanctions against Russia. Here is what you can do, from Ingolstadt.

1. Donate in Grossmehring

You can donate the following articles at the St. Wolfgang Church.

Baby nutrition/food.

Hygiene articles (like tooth paste, etc.)

Bedsheets

Kids’ clothes

Diapers and wet-wipes.

Address:Regensburgerstrasse 1, 85098, Großmehring.

2. The Entrepreneurs of Mooslhäusl

The entrepreneurs of Mooslhäusl in Ingolstadt have already collected the first donations to Ukraine.

Thanks to Markus Langer alias Sepp Bumsinger, a convoy with Donations is headed towards the Ukrainian Poland Border. You could help with the following articles.

If you want to donate, please try to do so in orderly packed cartons, bags, or sacks,with Markingssince repacking is a hassle.

Sleeping bags, isolation mats

Medicinal drugs and bandages

PET water bottles

Canned food

Gloves, beanies, shawls

Thermos flasks

Baby food, diapers, wet napkins, women’s hygiene articles,

Everything is also welcome in child-sizes.

You can drop your donations here during these times:

Address :

Im — Mooshäuslweg 1, 85049 Ingolstadt.

Timings:

Monday 15:00–20:00Uhr

Tuesday: 10:00–15:00 Uhr

If you want to donate money

Lookhere.

Follow their travels to Poland

here

3. The Ukrainian Catholic Community in München

The Catholic community in Munich is collecting the following articles for donation to Ukraine.

Isolation mats, small foldable mattresses, Sleeping bags, bedding material, and hygiene articles.

Medical equipment like bandages, blood bags, non-prescription painkillers, etc.

Groceries and food: Müslibars, fruit bars, chocolate, preserved/canned food.

If you are willing to take on a refugee, please contact the church shown at the address below.

If you want to donate, see the bank information on the screenshot below.

4. To Donate in other ways

Here are more places you can help with your donations.

See you in action! Thanks for your support!

I am Sruthi Korlakunta, living in Ingolstadt since 2019. I work full-time for Audi as a Data Analyst.

If you moved here recently, Welcome to Ingolstadt. Feel free to get in touch onLinkedIn. If you want to be a part of Medium,join here.

If you are new to the city, check outhttps://ingolstadtexpats.com/for some great contacts in the city. It is a blog run by and for expats in the town and has some great content to get started.

title: Pray for Afghan Women
content:
Pray for Afghan Women

“We will honor women’s rights within the norms of the Islamic law.”

“We will honor women’s rights within the norms of the Islamic law.”

The words of the Taliban Spokesperson rolling on one of my many open tabs. On another tab, Afghanistan’s national football playerfell to deathat the Kabul airport. He lost his hold while clinging to the bottom of a military airplane in flight in an attempt to escape.

One month ago, a woman was beaten to death with a Kalashnikov because she didn’t have anything to cook for a Taliban soldier. Her home was bombed as she lay in a pool of blood.

“We will honor women’s rights…”

As some women rush to tear down every poster and billboard with a picture of a woman on it,others destroy evidenceof working for the United States, shred the documents and bury cellphones. Memories of public amputations and executions 20 years ago have not yet faded.

Afghan women who helped the USA watch military planes depart without them.The paperwork is just not there.As the world and the UN abandon them, those women look to the sky for help that may not come.

“We will honor women’s rights…”

6000 thousand evacuations are currently planned for those who helped the US Military. Thousands more wait outside the Kabul airport, currently the most dangerous place in Afghanistan. As citizens risk their lives to flee, the Taliban expresses wonder as to why.

In a paradoxical display of tolerance, the Taliban spokesperson took questions from aWoman journalistand promised that women will be allowed to go to school and work. Hours later, a 28-year-old female journalist bore witness that all women were indefinitely suspended.

“We will honor women’s rights…”

They aligned themselves with American objectives, they bought into American initiatives, fully aware that that would make them targets in the eyes of insurgent groups like the Taliban. They did it anyway in the hope of a better future for the country, in the hope of a better future for the children, in the hope that the country would become more stable and more peaceful, more representative of all sections of Afghan society. I believe they were unbelievably courageous to do it.

So I want people to reach out to their representatives, to their leaders, and say, We have a moral obligation to those people, we have to evacuate those people. We cannot allow our partners — the U.S. has been calling the Afghan people “our partners” for 20 years — we cannot allow our partners to be murdered.

— Khaled Hosseini for the NY Times

The female journalists who have already lost meaning to their 20 years of education appear to be a precursor to what is really going to come. Women who wish to be anonymous express distrust and hopelessness about their rights under the new regime. Many don’t step out of their homes alone anymore for the fear of being killed. Others rush to find full-body burqas.

The Taliban has already shut down female-operated healthcare centers and prevented female students from entering university gates. Girls' schools have been shut down for months now. The UN Secretary-General has already reported “chilling accounts” of human rights violations across the country.

“We will honor women’s rights…”

Among the news that countries are expressing interest in doing business with the Taliban, where the USA and UK refuse responsibility for “citizens that are not their own”, we can only hope that the international community will persist in upholding Afghani civil liberty and human rights.

Harrowing images of personal possessions strewn across the street and gunshots fired into the crowd are being met with the indifference of the larger world.They are not our citizens, They are responsible for themselves starting 3…2…1…

Maybe the Taliban is really the rightful leader of these women,or maybe,the Nobel prize for going to school is just a token. If it is not, give those women a chance. Take a stand. Let them come to safer spaces. Call your representative and show your support to evacuate Afghan women.

title: Google’s Agile Project Management: Professional Certificate Coursera Review
content:
Google’s Agile Project Management: Professional Certificate Coursera Review

Review from a Data Analyst’s eye.

I recently tookGoogle’s Foundations in Project Management and Agile Project Management courseson Coursera. I am a Data Analyst in Sales at Audi in Germany. In all honesty, I am starved to find out about Agile methodologies and what they mean, people don’t seem to stop talking about it.

After the course, I understood that Agile is an incredibly popular and useful technique to manage projects in Software. But this trend is yet to catch on in other industries.

If you have been a Data consultant, analyst, and/or data scientist, a next career step to consider is a Product Owner and/or scrum master. For that, this course is relevant.

I took these modules in Project Management Professional Certificate from Google: Foundations in Project Management and Agile Project Management.

tl:dr;Take the course, it is good for starters. It is also useful in managing your personal projects and time. Don't expect to get a full-time job from it, but it is definitely a valid first step in building that career.

General Review

While you might take this course in the hope of securing a full-time job as a project manager, that probably won't happen.

You will not get a job as a project manager just by getting this certification. You need hands-on experience in managing small and big projects before you are given real major responsibilities.

Simply put, this course is“How to think as a Project Manager”.

It is comprehensive, beginner friendly, and informative. Many Project Managers and Scrum Masters from Google appear in the course and tell their stories. Not all of them began in the software industry. One lady also was a bar-tender turned Google Project Manager. This means the content is clearly targeted at beginners, which is a good thing.

The course is structured to suit modern attention spans and is not too demanding in its exercises and quick tests during the videos.

Review of module Foundations of Project Management

Foundations of Project Management (Suggested duration 4 weeks) took me 2 weeks (or less) of consistent effort to complete. It is beginner friendly and tells about the skeleton of a basic project.

The content for the first two weeks is basically about the job market in Project Management, what project managers do every day and how to think when about going about a project. It is a nice introduction to the topic, and almost story-like. I did this to set myself up for the Agile Project Management Course, the 5th module in the set of 6.

I did many chapters on the bus or the train. The effort isn’t massive and many parts are doable using only your mobile phone. It is like listening to a podcast and then answering questions.

Some exercises are, however, computer-only. You will get a certificate for the individual modules even if you don't take all 6 modules.

Review of Agile Project Management

This was the part I was looking forward to, and it delivered on its promise. It was a good course.

The instructor was an experienced Technical Program Manager at Google called Sue. She had a matter-of-fact tone and dealt with topics with detail and empathy. Her way of instruction made the course very enjoyable.

This course is also 4 weeks long, It took me 3. It is detail oriented and gives a thorough picture of the scrum with a case study on which you can practice.

It introduces the Agile Manifesto, Agile mindset, various roles in an Agile, and various tools to put all that in place.

It also gives you a hands-on experience with Asana, a Project Management tool where you can create a trial account and manage your case-study toy project.

It gives you a first overview of Kanban charts, Gant charts, and other popular project management tools to keep track of your work. The crux of the course is the Product Backlog, which you will create for the case study, prioritize and estimate.It was a fun activity and an incredibly effective teaching method.

Since the module is not purely theoretical (it is hands-on where you perform the role of Product owner and/or scrum master) you will remember the results for a long time.

If you are considering this course as a complete Agile-beginner, I recommend it. If you have already worked in an agile team and you want to deepen your knowledge about specifics of the methods, then this might be too shallow for you.

This is the first time I took a course on Coursera, and I am impressed. I subsequently enrolled in three other courses for fun- Intellectual Humility, the study of the Talmud, and Timeseries Forecasting using Scikit Learn.They do have a wide palette of courses.I am in the middle of my forecasting using Sci-kit learn, which I am also enjoying. The platform is awesome, it has changed the definition of learning for me.

My verdict for beginners — Take the course!

I am Sruthi Korlakunta. I write on lifestyle and analytics. Join me on Medium!
